toolRequests.name: memory
toolRequests.arguments:
{"command": "create", "path": "/memories/session/plan.md", "file_text": "# Plan: OM Design System — Figma Design System

## TL;DR
Build a production-grade Figma design system ("OM Design System") with full Light + Dark themes, modeled after MUI/Vibe/Ant quality. Uses Figma Variables for token management, atomic component architecture, and component properties for variants. Built across 5 phases, starting with foundations and scaling to full component coverage.

---

## Figma File Structure

Single Figma file with separate pages:

| Page | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| 🏠 Cover | Title card, version, changelog |
| 📐 Foundations / Tokens | Variables, colors, typography, spacing, elevation, grid |
| 🎨 Icons | 382 stroke icons from ZCat-Icons library |
| 🧱 Components | All UI components organized by frames |
| 📄 Patterns | Common layouts, forms, cards, dashboards |
| 📝 Documentation | Usage guidelines, do/don'ts per component |

---

## Phase 1: Foundations & Token Architecture (Do First)

### Step 1.1 — Figma Variables (Color Tokens)

Create a 3-tier variable system with **2 modes: Light / Dark**:

**Tier 1 — Primitives** (raw color values, not used directly):
- Blue scale: 50–950 (primary from `#2A65F0` light / `#3E78FF` dark)
- Violet scale: 50–950 (accent from `#8B5CF6` / `#7C3AED`)
- Cyan scale: 50–950 (secondary from `#06B6D4` / `#22D3EE`)
- Neutral scale: 50–950 (from existing `#101F3E` → `#F7F8FB`)
- Red/Green/Yellow/Orange for semantic colors
- Pure white, pure black, transparent

**Tier 2 — Semantic Tokens** (reference primitives, mode-aware):
| Token | Light | Dark |
|-------|-------|------|
| `bg/base` | #FFFFFF | #09090f |
| `bg/raised` | #FAFAFA | #0f1017 |
| `bg/card` | #FFFFFF | #14151f |
| `bg/surface` | rgba(0,0,0,0.03) | rgba(255,255,255,0.03) |
| `text/primary` | #101F3E | #E1E6F0 |
| `text/secondary` | #7988A8 | #8C9BB9 |
| `text/tertiary` | #888888 | #505268 |
| `text/muted` | #BBBBBB | #2e3044 |
| `border/default` | rgba(0,0,0,0.10) | rgba(255,255,255,0.08) |
| `border/strong` | rgba(0,0,0,0.18) | rgba(255,255,255,0.14) |
| `primary/default` | #2A65F0 | #3E78FF |
| `primary/foreground` | #FFFFFF | #FFFFFF |
| `accent/default` | #8B5CF6 | #A78BFA |
| `accent/glow` | rgba(139,92,246,0.15) | rgba(139,92,246,0.15) |
| `destructive/default` | #E22020 | #F85050 |
| `success/default` | #29B260 | #34C36E |
| `warning/default` | #F59E0B | #F59E0B |
| `overlay` | rgba(0,0,0,0.6) | rgba(0,0,0,0.6) |

**Tier 3 — Component Tokens** (optional, reference semantic):
- `button/primary/bg`, `button/primary/text`, `button/primary/border`
- `input/bg`, `input/border`, `input/placeholder`
- `card/bg`, `card/border`

### Step 1.2 — Typography Variables

Font: **Inter** (primary), **JetBrains Mono** (code)

| Style | Size | Weight | Line Height |
|-------|------|--------|-------------|
| Display | 48px | 700 | 56px |
| H1 | 40px | 700 | 48px |
| H2 | 32px | 600 | 40px |
| H3 | 24px | 600 | 32px |
| H4 | 20px | 600 | 28px |
| Body Large | 16px | 400 | 24px |
| Body / Base | 14px | 400 | 20px |
| Body Small | 13px | 400 | 18px |
| Label | 12px | 500 | 16px |
| Caption | 11px | 400 | 14px |

### Step 1.3 — Spacing Scale (Variables)

4px base unit: `0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 32, 40, 48, 64, 80, 96, 128`

### Step 1.4 — Border Radius (Variables)

| Token | Value |
|-------|-------|
| `radius/none` | 0px |
| `radius/sm` | 2px |
| `radius/md` | 4px |
| `radius/default` | 6px |
| `radius/lg` | 8px |
| `radius/xl` | 12px |
| `radius/2xl` | 16px |
| `radius/full` | 9999px |

### Step 1.5 — Elevation / Shadows (Effect Styles)

| Level | Light | Dark |
|-------|-------|------|
| `elevation/sm` | 0 4px 14px -4px rgba(239,242,250,1) | 0 4px 14px -4px rgba(0,0,0,0.4) |
| `elevation/md` | 0 8px 24px -8px rgba(0,0,0,0.08) | 0 8px 24px -8px rgba(0,0,0,0.5) |
| `elevation/lg` | 0 16px 48px -12px rgba(0,0,0,0.12) | 0 16px 48px -12px rgba(0,0,0,0.6) |
| `elevation/glow` | — | 0 0 20px rgba(139,92,246,0.15) |

### Step 1.6 — Grid System

- 12-column grid, 8px gutter
- Breakpoints: Mobile (375px), Tablet (768px), Desktop (1280px), Wide (1440px)

---

## Phase 2: Icons (Parallel with Phase 1)

### Step 2.1 — Import Icons
- Import all 382 stroke icons from ZCat-Icons as Figma components
- Organize into categories: General, Arrows, Dev, Commerce, Maps, Time, Media, Files, Users, Alerts, Communication, Devices, Security, Editor
- Each icon: 16×16 frame, stroke `currentColor` (use variable `text/primary`), stroke-width 1.3px
- Create component set with **size variants**: 12px, 16px, 20px, 24px

---

## Phase 3: Core Components

Build each component with:
- ✅ Auto Layout
- ✅ Component Properties (boolean, instance swap, text)
- ✅ Variant combinations via Component Sets
- ✅ All states: Default, Hover, Active/Pressed, Focus, Disabled
- ✅ Both Light/Dark via variable binding (automatic)

### 3.1 — Button
**Variants**: Primary, Secondary, Outline, Ghost, Destructive, Link
**Sizes**: Small (32px), Medium (36px), Large (40px)
**Properties**: Label (text), Left Icon (instance swap), Right Icon (instance swap), Icon Only (boolean), Loading (boolean), Disabled (boolean)
**States**: Default, Hover, Active, Focus, Disabled, Loading

### 3.2 — Input / TextField
**Variants**: Default, Filled, Outline
**Sizes**: Small, Medium, Large
**Properties**: Label (text), Placeholder (text), Helper Text (text), Error (boolean), Left Icon (instance swap), Right Icon (instance swap), Clearable (boolean)
**States**: Default, Hover, Focus, Error, Disabled, Read-only

### 3.3 — Select / Dropdown
**Properties**: Label, Placeholder, Multi-select (boolean), Searchable (boolean), Error (boolean)
**Includes**: Dropdown menu, Option item (with checkbox for multi), Option group

### 3.4 — Checkbox
**Sizes**: Small, Medium
**States**: Unchecked, Checked, Indeterminate, Disabled
**Properties**: Label (text), Description (text)

### 3.5 — Radio
**Sizes**: Small, Medium
**States**: Unselected, Selected, Disabled
**Properties**: Label (text)

### 3.6 — Toggle / Switch
**Sizes**: Small, Medium
**States**: Off, On, Disabled
**Properties**: Label (text)

### 3.7 — DatePicker (Complex)
**Includes**: Calendar grid, Month/Year navigation, Date cell, Range selection
**Defer to Phase 5 if time-constrained**

---

## Phase 4: Data Display + Feedback + Navigation

### Data Display
- **Avatar** — Sizes (24/32/40/48), Image/Initials/Icon, Status badge, Group
- **Badge** — Variants (filled, outline, subtle), Colors (primary, success, warning, error, neutral), Sizes (sm, md)
- **Tag / Chip** — Removable (boolean), Icon (instance swap), Colors
- **Card** — Header, Body, Footer slots, Elevated/Outlined variants
- **Table** — Header row, Body row, Cell variants, Sortable, Selectable, Pagination
- **Tooltip** — Placement (top/bottom/left/right), Arrow (boolean)

### Feedback
- **Alert** — Types (info, success, warning, error), Title + Description, Dismissable, Action button
- **Toast / Snackbar** — Position variants, Auto-dismiss, Action
- **Dialog / Modal** — Sizes (sm/md/lg/full), Header/Body/Footer, Close button
- **Progress** — Linear, Circular, Indeterminate
- **Skeleton** — Text, Avatar, Card, Table row variants
- **Spinner** — Sizes (sm/md/lg)

### Navigation
- **Tabs** — Underline, Contained, Pill variants; Horizontal/Vertical
- **Sidebar** — Collapsible, Nested items, Icon + Label, Active state
- **Breadcrumb** — Separator variants, Truncation
- **Pagination** — Numbered, Previous/Next, Items per page
- **Menu / Dropdown Menu** — Items, Separators, Nested, Icons, Keyboard shortcut hints

---

## Phase 5: Patterns & Documentation

### 5.1 — Pattern Templates
- Form layouts (single column, two column, inline)
- Dashboard card grid
- Data table with filters
- Empty states
- Error pages (404, 500)
- Auth screens (login, signup)

### 5.2 — Documentation Page
- Per-component: anatomy diagram, variant showcase, do/don't examples
- Token reference sheet (all colors, typography, spacing visualized)

---

## Recommended Figma Plugins & Tools

| Plugin | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| **Tokens Studio** | Sync design tokens to JSON, manage variables at scale |
| **Iconify** | Quick icon import fallback |
| **Contrast** | WCAG accessibility contrast checking |
| **Batch Styler** | Bulk edit text/color styles |
| **Content Reel** | Realistic placeholder content |

---

## Execution Strategy (Fast Track)

| Phase | Effort | Can Parallelize? |
|-------|--------|-----------------|
| Phase 1: Foundations | ~1 day | Phase 2 runs parallel |
| Phase 2: Icons | ~0.5 day | Parallel with Phase 1 |
| Phase 3: Core Components | ~2–3 days | Sequential (depends on Phase 1) |
| Phase 4: Data/Feedback/Nav | ~2–3 days | Sequential (depends on Phase 3 atoms) |
| Phase 5: Patterns & Docs | ~1 day | After Phase 4 |

### Speed Tips
1. **Use Figma's "Generate all combinations"** when creating component sets — auto-generates variant grid
2. **Build one reference component fully** (Button), then use it as a template for all others
3. **Bind variables to components** rather than hard-coding hex — instant Light/Dark support
4. **Prefix internal/atom components with `.` or `_`** to hide from publish
5. **Use MCP Figma server** (already configured) to programmatically create variables and components via API if needed

---

## Decisions
- **Token source**: Merging the best of SlateAI-test (light theme) + om (dark theme) token sets for a unified system
- **Icon source**: Reusing all 382 ZCat-Icons (stroke style, 16×16, 1.3px stroke)
- **Naming convention**: slash-based hierarchy (e.g., `Button/Primary/Medium/Default`)
- **Scope**: Figma design system only (no code library in this plan)
- **Excluded**: Animation/motion specs, mobile-specific components, charting components

---

## Relevant Files (Token Reference)
- `SlateAI-test/src/styles/theme.css` — Light/dark color tokens, spacing, radius, elevation
- `om/src/index.css` — Dark-first surface tokens, glow effects, accent colors
- `ZCat-Icons/ICON_STYLE_GUIDE.md` — Icon specifications (16×16, stroke 1.3px, #101F3E)

---

## Verification
1. Toggle Light ↔ Dark mode on every component — verify all colors switch correctly via variables
2. Run Figma accessibility plugin (Contrast) on all text/bg combinations in both themes
3. Test all component states render correctly in prototypes
4. Verify icon components scale properly at all 4 sizes
5. Test component instances: override text, swap icons, toggle booleans — confirm no breaking
6. Publish library and enable in a test file — verify all components appear in Assets panel
"}

data.content:
Here's the plan:

---

## Plan: OM Design System (Figma)

Build a production-grade Figma design system with full Light + Dark themes, modeled after MUI/Vibe/Ant quality. Uses Figma Variables for token management, atomic component architecture, and component properties for variants.

---

### Figma File Structure

| Page | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| 🏠 Cover | Title card, version, changelog |
| 📐 Foundations | Variables, colors, typography, spacing, elevation, grid |
| 🎨 Icons | 382 stroke icons from your ZCat-Icons library |
| 🧱 Components | All UI components organized by frames |
| 📄 Patterns | Common layouts, forms, dashboards |
| 📝 Documentation | Usage guidelines, do/don'ts |

---

### Phase 1: Foundations & Token Architecture *(Do First)*

**Step 1.1 — Color Variables (3-tier system, Light/Dark modes)**

- **Tier 1 — Primitives**: Raw color scales (Blue, Violet, Cyan, Neutral, Red, Green, Yellow) from 50–950. Sourced from your existing tokens (`#2A65F0` primary, `#8B5CF6` accent, `#06B6D4` secondary).
- **Tier 2 — Semantic Tokens** (mode-aware, auto-switch Light↔Dark):

| Token | Light | Dark |
|-------|-------|------|
| `bg/base` | #FFFFFF | #09090f |
| `bg/raised` | #FAFAFA | #0f1017 |
| `bg/card` | #FFFFFF | #14151f |
| `text/primary` | #101F3E | #E1E6F0 |
| `text/secondary` | #7988A8 | #8C9BB9 |
| `border/default` | rgba(0,0,0,0.10) | rgba(255,255,255,0.08) |
| `primary/default` | #2A65F0 | #3E78FF |
| `accent/default` | #8B5CF6 | #A78BFA |
| `destructive` | #E22020 | #F85050 |
| `success` | #29B260 | #34C36E |
| `accent/glow` | — | rgba(139,92,246,0.15) |

- **Tier 3 — Component Tokens**: `button/primary/bg`, `input/bg`, `card/bg` etc. (reference semantic tokens)

**Step 1.2 — Typography**: Inter + JetBrains Mono. 10 styles from Display (48px) → Caption (11px).

**Step 1.3 — Spacing**: 4px base unit scale: `0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 32, 40, 48, 64, 80, 96, 128`

**Step 1.4 — Border Radius**: `none(0)` → `sm(2)` → `md(4)` → `default(6)` → `lg(8)` → `xl(12)` → `2xl(16)` → `full(9999)`

**Step 1.5 — Elevation/Shadows**: 4 levels (sm, md, lg, glow) — glow effect for dark theme futuristic feel.

**Step 1.6 — Grid**: 12-column, 8px gutter. Breakpoints: 375 / 768 / 1280 / 1440px.

---

### Phase 2: Icons *(Parallel with Phase 1)*

- Import all **382 stroke icons** from your ZCat-Icons library
- Organize into 15 categories (General, Arrows, Dev, Commerce, etc.)
- Component set with **4 size variants**: 12, 16, 20, 24px
- Bind stroke color to `text/primary` variable → auto Light/Dark

---

### Phase 3: Core Components

Every component gets: **Auto Layout + Component Properties + All States + Variable-bound colors** (instant Light/Dark for free).

| Component | Variants | Sizes | Key Properties |
|-----------|----------|-------|----------------|
| **Button** | Primary, Secondary, Outline, Ghost, Destructive, Link | S/M/L | Label, Left/Right Icon, Icon-only, Loading, Disabled |
| **Input** | Default, Filled, Outline | S/M/L | Label, Placeholder, Helper, Error, Icons, Clearable |
| **Select** | Single, Multi, Searchable | S/M/L | Label, Error, Options list |
| **Checkbox** | Unchecked, Checked, Indeterminate | S/M | Label, Description, Disabled |
| **Radio** | Unselected, Selected | S/M | Label, Disabled |
| **Toggle** | Off, On | S/M | Label, Disabled |

**States for all**: Default → Hover → Active → Focus → Disabled

---

### Phase 4: Data Display + Feedback + Navigation

**Data Display**: Avatar (4 sizes + status), Badge, Tag/Chip (removable), Card, Table (sortable/selectable), Tooltip

**Feedback**: Alert (info/success/warning/error), Toast, Dialog/Modal (sm/md/lg), Progress (linear/circular), Skeleton, Spinner

**Navigation**: Tabs (underline/contained/pill), Sidebar (collapsible/nested), Breadcrumb, Pagination, Menu/Dropdown

---

### Phase 5: Patterns & Documentation

- Form layouts, Dashboard card grids, Empty states, Error pages, Auth screens
- Per-component anatomy diagrams and do/don't examples

---

### Speed Strategy

| Trick | Impact |
|-------|--------|
| Use Figma **"Generate all combinations"** | Auto-creates variant grids |
| Build Button first as the template | Copy pattern to all components |
| Bind **Variables** not hex codes | Instant dual-theme support |
| Prefix atoms with `.` or `_` | Hide internals from publish |
| Use **Figma MCP server** (already set up) | Programmatically create variables/components via API |
| Use **Tokens Studio plugin** | Bulk manage & export tokens |

---

### Execution Order

| Phase | Depends On | Parallelizable? |
|-------|-----------|-----------------|
| 1: Foundations | — | Phase 2 parallel |
| 2: Icons | — | Phase 1 parallel |
| 3: Core Components | Phase 1 | Sequential |
| 4: Data/Feedback/Nav | Phase 3 (reuses atoms) | Sequential |
| 5: Patterns & Docs | Phase 4 | After all |

---

### Decisions Made
- **Token source**: Merged from your SlateAI-test (light) + om (dark) existing tokens
- **Icons**: Reusing 382 ZCat-Icons (stroke, 16×16, 1.3px)
- **Naming**: Slash hierarchy (`Button/Primary/Medium/Default`)
- **Scope**: Figma only — no code library
- **Excluded**: Animation/motion specs, mobile-native components, chart components

### Verification
1. Toggle Light ↔ Dark on every component — all colors must switch via variables
2. Run Contrast plugin — WCAG AA on all text/bg combos in both themes
3. Test overrides: swap icons, edit text, toggle booleans — no breaking
4. Publish library → enable in test file → confirm all assets appear

---

Ready to start? Let me know if you want to adjust scope, reorder phases, or if I should begin building via the Figma MCP server.

data.reasoningText:
Now I have clear direction:

1. **Primary**: `#FD6303` (orange) — Light mode primary
2. **Alt Theme**: `#2A65F0` (blue) — Separate switchable theme
3. **Neutrals**: Cool neutrals (blue-grey tones)
4. **Dark Mode**: Softer/pastel orange for dark mode (something like `#FFB066`)
5. **Theme Switching**: Two separate accent themes (Orange / Blue)
6. **Secondary**: Derived from primary (lighter/darker shades)

Let me design proper color scales for both themes and update the plan.


I'm building out the full color scales now—starting with the orange palette that ranges from a very light cream at the lightest end through to deep brown tones, with the primary orange sitting at the midpoint. For dark mode, I'll use a softer peachy tone instead of the harsh primary. The blue scale will follow a similar structure. Finishing out the cool neutral palette with the darker shades, then I'll revise my approach.

{"agentName": "Explore", "description": "Explore ZCat variable structure", "prompt": "I need to understand the ZCatalyst Design System's Figma variable structure from the screenshot context. The user showed a Figma Variables panel with these collections and groups:

Collections:
- Mode: 493 variables
- Typography: 55 variables
- Theme: 10 variables
- _Global_Colors: 111 variables
- _Global_Values: 16 variables

Groups visible:
- BODY (16): Background (2), Static (2), Border (2), Static (2), Text (6), Static (6), Icons (6), Static (6)
- CARDS (22)
- SHADOWS (2)
- OTHER SHADES (28): Red (4 shades each with Light/Dark), Green (4 shades), Theme (4 shades), Blue (4 shades)
- BUTTONS (87): Fill (17) → Background (12), Text & Icon (4), Split (1); Outline (27) → Text & Icons (12), Borders (12), Bg (3); Grey (12)

Each variable has Light and Dark mode columns, and references aliases like `_Light-Colors/_Red/Red-1` and `_Dark-Colors/_Red/Red-1`.

Based on this structure, and looking at how MUI, Vibe (monday.com), and Ant Design organize their Figma variables, research and report back:

1. What would be the COMPLETE list of groups and sub-groups needed for a full design system? Think about EVERY component that needs its own color group. List them all with estimated variable counts.
2. What's the typical naming convention for these per-component variables?
3. How do the _Global_Colors typically map to the Mode collection? (i.e., how does the aliasing chain work?)

Search the web if needed for MUI Figma variable structure, Ant Design Figma tokens, or monday.com Vibe design system Figma variables. Focus on the per-component variable breakdown.

Be thorough - I need every single group and sub-group."}

data.reasoningText:
The user is showing me a screenshot from what appears to be the ZCatalyst Design System's Figma Variables panel. I can see they have a sophisticated variable structure with:

- **Collections**: Mode (493), Typography (55), Theme (10), _Global_Colors (111), _Global_Values (16)
- **Groups**: All (493), BODY (16), CARDS (22), SHADOWS (2), OTHER SHADES (28), BUTTONS (87), etc.
- **Structure**: Variables are organized per-component with Light/Dark modes
- **OTHER SHADES** section shows: Red (4 shades), Green (4 shades), Theme (4 shades), Blue (4 shades)


- **Aliases**: They're using nested references like `_Light-Colors/_Red/Red-1` and `_Dark-Colors/_Red/Red-1`

Now I'm understanding what they need: a component-level color token system where each UI element (Button, Card, Input, etc.) has its own dedicated color variables, not just semantic tokens. They want to use their brand colors—an orange primary (#FD6303) and blue theme (#2A65F0)—with two switchable accent themes, cool neutrals, a softer pastel primary for dark mode, and secondary colors derived from the primary. I'm going to design a comprehensive variable architecture that mirrors the structure they've shown but tailored to their specific color palette and component needs.

The full structure should include collections for raw primitives (orange, blue, neutral scales plus accent colors), global semantic aliases for light and dark modes, component-level tokens organized by UI element with their own light/dark variants, theme switching between orange and blue, plus typography and spacing systems. For each component like BODY, CARDS, and BUTTONS, I need to define specific variables for backgrounds, borders, text, shadows, and other properties. selected border, progress track and fill, skeleton shimmer effects, pagination states, breadcrumb navigation, menu interactions, and tag variants. Now I'm thinking through the primary orange color #FD6303 and building out the complete palette—lighter tints for backgrounds and hover states in light mode, darker shades for text contrast, and a softer variant like #FFB066 for dark mode applications.

data.content: Now I have a complete picture. Let me update the plan with the full per-component variable architecture using your actual colors.

{"command": "str_replace", "path": "/memories/session/plan.md", "old_str": "# Plan: OM Design System — Figma Design System

## TL;DR
Build a production-grade Figma design system ("OM Design System") with full Light + Dark themes, modeled after MUI/Vibe/Ant quality. Uses Figma Variables for token management, atomic component architecture, and component properties for variants. Built across 5 phases, starting with foundations and scaling to full component coverage.

---

## Figma File Structure

Single Figma file with separate pages:

| Page | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| 🏠 Cover | Title card, version, changelog |
| 📐 Foundations / Tokens | Variables, colors, typography, spacing, elevation, grid |
| 🎨 Icons | 382 stroke icons from ZCat-Icons library |
| 🧱 Components | All UI components organized by frames |
| 📄 Patterns | Common layouts, forms, cards, dashboards |
| 📝 Documentation | Usage guidelines, do/don'ts per component |

---

## Phase 1: Foundations & Token Architecture (Do First)

### Step 1.1 — Figma Variables (Color Tokens)

Create a 3-tier variable system with **2 modes: Light / Dark**:

**Tier 1 — Primitives** (raw color values, not used directly):
- Blue scale: 50–950 (primary from `#2A65F0` light / `#3E78FF` dark)
- Violet scale: 50–950 (accent from `#8B5CF6` / `#7C3AED`)
- Cyan scale: 50–950 (secondary from `#06B6D4` / `#22D3EE`)
- Neutral scale: 50–950 (from existing `#101F3E` → `#F7F8FB`)
- Red/Green/Yellow/Orange for semantic colors
- Pure white, pure black, transparent

**Tier 2 — Semantic Tokens** (reference primitives, mode-aware):
| Token | Light | Dark |
|-------|-------|------|
| `bg/base` | #FFFFFF | #09090f |
| `bg/raised` | #FAFAFA | #0f1017 |
| `bg/card` | #FFFFFF | #14151f |
| `bg/surface` | rgba(0,0,0,0.03) | rgba(255,255,255,0.03) |
| `text/primary` | #101F3E | #E1E6F0 |
| `text/secondary` | #7988A8 | #8C9BB9 |
| `text/tertiary` | #888888 | #505268 |
| `text/muted` | #BBBBBB | #2e3044 |
| `border/default` | rgba(0,0,0,0.10) | rgba(255,255,255,0.08) |
| `border/strong` | rgba(0,0,0,0.18) | rgba(255,255,255,0.14) |
| `primary/default` | #2A65F0 | #3E78FF |
| `primary/foreground` | #FFFFFF | #FFFFFF |
| `accent/default` | #8B5CF6 | #A78BFA |
| `accent/glow` | rgba(139,92,246,0.15) | rgba(139,92,246,0.15) |
| `destructive/default` | #E22020 | #F85050 |
| `success/default` | #29B260 | #34C36E |
| `warning/default` | #F59E0B | #F59E0B |
| `overlay` | rgba(0,0,0,0.6) | rgba(0,0,0,0.6) |

**Tier 3 — Component Tokens** (optional, reference semantic):
- `button/primary/bg`, `button/primary/text`, `button/primary/border`
- `input/bg`, `input/border`, `input/placeholder`
- `card/bg`, `card/border`

### Step 1.2 — Typography Variables

Font: **Inter** (primary), **JetBrains Mono** (code)

| Style | Size | Weight | Line Height |
|-------|------|--------|-------------|
| Display | 48px | 700 | 56px |
| H1 | 40px | 700 | 48px |
| H2 | 32px | 600 | 40px |
| H3 | 24px | 600 | 32px |
| H4 | 20px | 600 | 28px |
| Body Large | 16px | 400 | 24px |
| Body / Base | 14px | 400 | 20px |
| Body Small | 13px | 400 | 18px |
| Label | 12px | 500 | 16px |
| Caption | 11px | 400 | 14px |

### Step 1.3 — Spacing Scale (Variables)

4px base unit: `0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 32, 40, 48, 64, 80, 96, 128`

### Step 1.4 — Border Radius (Variables)

| Token | Value |
|-------|-------|
| `radius/none` | 0px |
| `radius/sm` | 2px |
| `radius/md` | 4px |
| `radius/default` | 6px |
| `radius/lg` | 8px |
| `radius/xl` | 12px |
| `radius/2xl` | 16px |
| `radius/full` | 9999px |

### Step 1.5 — Elevation / Shadows (Effect Styles)

| Level | Light | Dark |
|-------|-------|------|
| `elevation/sm` | 0 4px 14px -4px rgba(239,242,250,1) | 0 4px 14px -4px rgba(0,0,0,0.4) |
| `elevation/md` | 0 8px 24px -8px rgba(0,0,0,0.08) | 0 8px 24px -8px rgba(0,0,0,0.5) |
| `elevation/lg` | 0 16px 48px -12px rgba(0,0,0,0.12) | 0 16px 48px -12px rgba(0,0,0,0.6) |
| `elevation/glow` | — | 0 0 20px rgba(139,92,246,0.15) |

### Step 1.6 — Grid System

- 12-column grid, 8px gutter
- Breakpoints: Mobile (375px), Tablet (768px), Desktop (1280px), Wide (1440px)

---

## Phase 2: Icons (Parallel with Phase 1)

### Step 2.1 — Import Icons
- Import all 382 stroke icons from ZCat-Icons as Figma components
- Organize into categories: General, Arrows, Dev, Commerce, Maps, Time, Media, Files, Users, Alerts, Communication, Devices, Security, Editor
- Each icon: 16×16 frame, stroke `currentColor` (use variable `text/primary`), stroke-width 1.3px
- Create component set with **size variants**: 12px, 16px, 20px, 24px

---

## Phase 3: Core Components

Build each component with:
- ✅ Auto Layout
- ✅ Component Properties (boolean, instance swap, text)
- ✅ Variant combinations via Component Sets
- ✅ All states: Default, Hover, Active/Pressed, Focus, Disabled
- ✅ Both Light/Dark via variable binding (automatic)

### 3.1 — Button
**Variants**: Primary, Secondary, Outline, Ghost, Destructive, Link
**Sizes**: Small (32px), Medium (36px), Large (40px)
**Properties**: Label (text), Left Icon (instance swap), Right Icon (instance swap), Icon Only (boolean), Loading (boolean), Disabled (boolean)
**States**: Default, Hover, Active, Focus, Disabled, Loading

### 3.2 — Input / TextField
**Variants**: Default, Filled, Outline
**Sizes**: Small, Medium, Large
**Properties**: Label (text), Placeholder (text), Helper Text (text), Error (boolean), Left Icon (instance swap), Right Icon (instance swap), Clearable (boolean)
**States**: Default, Hover, Focus, Error, Disabled, Read-only

### 3.3 — Select / Dropdown
**Properties**: Label, Placeholder, Multi-select (boolean), Searchable (boolean), Error (boolean)
**Includes**: Dropdown menu, Option item (with checkbox for multi), Option group

### 3.4 — Checkbox
**Sizes**: Small, Medium
**States**: Unchecked, Checked, Indeterminate, Disabled
**Properties**: Label (text), Description (text)

### 3.5 — Radio
**Sizes**: Small, Medium
**States**: Unselected, Selected, Disabled
**Properties**: Label (text)

### 3.6 — Toggle / Switch
**Sizes**: Small, Medium
**States**: Off, On, Disabled
**Properties**: Label (text)

### 3.7 — DatePicker (Complex)
**Includes**: Calendar grid, Month/Year navigation, Date cell, Range selection
**Defer to Phase 5 if time-constrained**

---

## Phase 4: Data Display + Feedback + Navigation

### Data Display
- **Avatar** — Sizes (24/32/40/48), Image/Initials/Icon, Status badge, Group
- **Badge** — Variants (filled, outline, subtle), Colors (primary, success, warning, error, neutral), Sizes (sm, md)
- **Tag / Chip** — Removable (boolean), Icon (instance swap), Colors
- **Card** — Header, Body, Footer slots, Elevated/Outlined variants
- **Table** — Header row, Body row, Cell variants, Sortable, Selectable, Pagination
- **Tooltip** — Placement (top/bottom/left/right), Arrow (boolean)

### Feedback
- **Alert** — Types (info, success, warning, error), Title + Description, Dismissable, Action button
- **Toast / Snackbar** — Position variants, Auto-dismiss, Action
- **Dialog / Modal** — Sizes (sm/md/lg/full), Header/Body/Footer, Close button
- **Progress** — Linear, Circular, Indeterminate
- **Skeleton** — Text, Avatar, Card, Table row variants
- **Spinner** — Sizes (sm/md/lg)

### Navigation
- **Tabs** — Underline, Contained, Pill variants; Horizontal/Vertical
- **Sidebar** — Collapsible, Nested items, Icon + Label, Active state
- **Breadcrumb** — Separator variants, Truncation
- **Pagination** — Numbered, Previous/Next, Items per page
- **Menu / Dropdown Menu** — Items, Separators, Nested, Icons, Keyboard shortcut hints

---

## Phase 5: Patterns & Documentation

### 5.1 — Pattern Templates
- Form layouts (single column, two column, inline)
- Dashboard card grid
- Data table with filters
- Empty states
- Error pages (404, 500)
- Auth screens (login, signup)

### 5.2 — Documentation Page
- Per-component: anatomy diagram, variant showcase, do/don't examples
- Token reference sheet (all colors, typography, spacing visualized)

---

## Recommended Figma Plugins & Tools

| Plugin | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| **Tokens Studio** | Sync design tokens to JSON, manage variables at scale |
| **Iconify** | Quick icon import fallback |
| **Contrast** | WCAG accessibility contrast checking |
| **Batch Styler** | Bulk edit text/color styles |
| **Content Reel** | Realistic placeholder content |

---

## Execution Strategy (Fast Track)

| Phase | Effort | Can Parallelize? |
|-------|--------|-----------------|
| Phase 1: Foundations | ~1 day | Phase 2 runs parallel |
| Phase 2: Icons | ~0.5 day | Parallel with Phase 1 |
| Phase 3: Core Components | ~2–3 days | Sequential (depends on Phase 1) |
| Phase 4: Data/Feedback/Nav | ~2–3 days | Sequential (depends on Phase 3 atoms) |
| Phase 5: Patterns & Docs | ~1 day | After Phase 4 |

### Speed Tips
1. **Use Figma's "Generate all combinations"** when creating component sets — auto-generates variant grid
2. **Build one reference component fully** (Button), then use it as a template for all others
3. **Bind variables to components** rather than hard-coding hex — instant Light/Dark support
4. **Prefix internal/atom components with `.` or `_`** to hide from publish
5. **Use MCP Figma server** (already configured) to programmatically create variables and components via API if needed

---

## Decisions
- **Token source**: Merging the best of SlateAI-test (light theme) + om (dark theme) token sets for a unified system
- **Icon source**: Reusing all 382 ZCat-Icons (stroke style, 16×16, 1.3px stroke)
- **Naming convention**: slash-based hierarchy (e.g., `Button/Primary/Medium/Default`)
- **Scope**: Figma design system only (no code library in this plan)
- **Excluded**: Animation/motion specs, mobile-specific components, charting components

---

## Relevant Files (Token Reference)
- `SlateAI-test/src/styles/theme.css` — Light/dark color tokens, spacing, radius, elevation
- `om/src/index.css` — Dark-first surface tokens, glow effects, accent colors
- `ZCat-Icons/ICON_STYLE_GUIDE.md` — Icon specifications (16×16, stroke 1.3px, #101F3E)

---

## Verification
1. Toggle Light ↔ Dark mode on every component — verify all colors switch correctly via variables
2. Run Figma accessibility plugin (Contrast) on all text/bg combinations in both themes
3. Test all component states render correctly in prototypes
4. Verify icon components scale properly at all 4 sizes
5. Test component instances: override text, swap icons, toggle booleans — confirm no breaking
6. Publish library and enable in a test file — verify all components appear in Assets panel", "new_str": "# Plan: OM Design System — Figma Design System (v2)

## Figma File Structure

| Page | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| 🏠 Cover | Title card, version, changelog |
| 📐 Foundations | Color palette showcase, typography, spacing, elevation, grid |
| 🎨 Icons | 382 ZCat stroke icons |
| 🧱 Components | All UI components organized by frames |
| 📄 Patterns | Common layouts |
| 📝 Documentation | Usage guidelines, do/don'ts |

---

## Variable Collections Architecture (~900+ variables)

### Collection 1: `_Primitives` (~120 vars)
Raw color values. Never used directly on components.

Groups:
- `_Orange/` — 50 through 900 (10 values)
- `_Blue/` — 50 through 900 (10 values)
- `_Neutral/` — 0 through 950 (13 values)
- `_Red/` — 4 shades (Red-1 to Red-4)
- `_Green/` — 4 shades (Green-1 to Green-4)
- `_Yellow/` — 4 shades (Yellow-1 to Yellow-4)
- `_Cyan/` — 4 shades (Cyan-1 to Cyan-4)
- `_White`, `_Black`, `_Transparent`

Modes: **Light, Dark** (values swap per mode)

---

### Collection 2: `Theme` (~10 vars)
Switchable accent. Modes: **Ember (Orange), Ocean (Blue)**

| Variable | Ember | Ocean |
|----------|-------|-------|
| Primary/1 | → _Orange/500 | → _Blue/500 |
| Primary/2 | → _Orange/400 | → _Blue/400 |
| Primary/3 | → _Orange/300 | → _Blue/300 |
| Primary/4 | → _Orange/100 | → _Blue/100 |
| Primary/Foreground | → _White | → _White |

---

## Component Build Order

### Phase 1 — Variables & Foundations
1. Create `_Primitives` collection with all color scales
2. Create `Theme` collection (Ember/Ocean modes)
3. Create `Mode` collection with ALL component groups above
4. Create `Typography` and `_Global_Values` collections
5. Build foundation showcase page (color grid, type scale, spacing ruler, shadow cards)

### Phase 2 — Icons (*parallel with Phase 1*)
- Import 382 ZCat stroke icons as components
- 4 size variants: 12, 16, 20, 24px
- Bind stroke to `Body/Icons/Primary` variable

### Phase 3 — Core Components (depends on Phase 1)
Build in this order (each uses its own variable group):
1. **Button** — uses `BUTTONS/*` vars. Primary template component.
2. **Input** — uses `INPUT/*` vars
3. **Select** — uses `SELECT/*` vars
4. **Checkbox** — uses `CHECKBOX/*` vars
5. **Radio** — uses `RADIO/*` vars
6. **Toggle** — uses `TOGGLE/*` vars

### Phase 4 — Data Display + Feedback + Navigation (depends on Phase 3)
1. **Card** → `CARDS/*`
2. **Table** → `TABLE/*`
3. **Avatar** → `AVATAR/*`
4. **Badge** → `BADGE/*`
5. **Tag** → `TAG/*`
6. **Tooltip** → `TOOLTIP/*`
7. **Alert** → `ALERT/*`
8. **Toast** → `TOAST/*`
9. **Modal** → `MODAL/*`
10. **Progress** → `PROGRESS/*`
11. **Skeleton** → `SKELETON/*`
12. **Spinner** → `SPINNER/*`
13. **Tabs** → `TABS/*`
14. **Sidebar** → `SIDEBAR/*`
15. **Breadcrumb** → `BREADCRUMB/*`
16. **Pagination** → `PAGINATION/*`
17. **Menu** → `MENU/*`

### Phase 5 — Patterns & Documentation
- Form layouts, Dashboard grids, Empty states
- Per-component do/don't sheets

---

## Decisions
- Primary: #FD6303 (Orange/Ember theme), #2A65F0 (Blue/Ocean theme)
- Dark mode primary: Softer/pastel (#FFB066 for orange, #7DA2F5 for blue)
- Neutrals: Cool (blue-grey tint)
- Secondary: Derived from lighter/darker shades of primary (no separate accent color)
- Two switchable themes via Figma variable modes (Ember/Ocean)
- Per-component variable groups — every component has its own dedicated color variables
- Icons: 382 ZCat stroke icons, 16×16, 1.3px stroke
- Scope: Figma only, no code
- Excluded: Animation, mobile-native, charts, DatePicker (deferred)

## Verification
1. Switch Theme mode (Ember↔Ocean) — all components update primary colors
2. Switch Light↔Dark — all surfaces, text, borders invert correctly
3. All 4 combinations work: Ember+Light, Ember+Dark, Ocean+Light, Ocean+Dark
4. Contrast plugin: WCAG AA on all text/bg combos across all 4 modes
5. Test overrides: swap icons, edit text, toggle booleans — no breaking
6. Publish → enable in test file → all assets appear"}

{"command": "create", "path": "/memories/session/plan.md", "file_text": "# Plan: OM Design System (Figma) — Fresh Build, Guided Step-by-Step

## TL;DR
Build a fresh Figma design system "OM Design System" with **per-component color variables** (like ZCatalyst structure), **strict atomic composition** (every component uses other components — never duplicates), and **theme switching** between Orange (#FD6303) and Blue (#2A65F0) with Light/Dark modes for each. Built one component at a time, in guided order.

---

## Core Principles (Non-Negotiable)

1. **Atomic Composition**: Every higher-order component is built from instances of lower-order components.
   - Multi-select dropdown's checkbox → instance of Checkbox component
   - Modal's button → instance of Button component
   - Table's button → instance of Button component
   - Change Button padding once → propagates everywhere it's used
2. **Icons ONLY from Icon component**: No custom SVGs inside components — every icon is an instance from the Icon library
3. **Per-component variables**: Each component owns its color tokens (not shared/global) so we can tweak without breaking others
4. **Variable-bound everything**: No hard-coded hex; all colors reference variables → instant theme switch

---

## Variable Architecture (3-Tier + 4-Mode)

### Collections
| Collection | Modes | Purpose |
|------------|-------|---------|
| `_Primitives` | None | Raw color/value scales (source of truth) |
| `_Semantic` | Orange-Light, Orange-Dark, Blue-Light, Blue-Dark | Mode-aware aliases |
| `Component/*` | (inherits from Semantic) | Per-component color tokens |
| `Typography` | None | Font family, size, weight, line-height |
| `Spacing` | None | Spacing scale |
| `Radius` | None | Border radius scale |
| `Shadows` | Light, Dark | Elevation effects |

### Tier 1 — `_Primitives` Collection
Raw scales (50–950 for each), **fresh palette derived from your two brand colors**:

- **Orange scale** (anchored at #FD6303 = 600)
- **Blue scale** (anchored at #2A65F0 = 600)
- **Cool Neutral scale** (blue-grey tinted, 50–950) — chosen for cool neutral pairing
- **Red** (destructive), **Green** (success), **Amber** (warning), **Sky** (info) — semantic scales
- **Pure**: White, Black, Transparent

### Tier 2 — `_Semantic` Collection (4 Modes)

Single collection, 4 modes: `Orange-Light`, `Orange-Dark`, `Blue-Light`, `Blue-Dark`. Each token resolves differently per mode.

**Surface tokens:**
- `surface/base`, `surface/raised`, `surface/sunken`, `surface/card`, `surface/overlay`

**Text tokens:**
- `text/primary`, `text/secondary`, `text/tertiary`, `text/disabled`, `text/inverse`, `text/on-brand`

**Border tokens:**
- `border/subtle`, `border/default`, `border/strong`, `border/focus`

**Brand tokens** (the actual theme switch):
- `brand/primary` → Orange-Light: #FD6303 / Orange-Dark: #FFB066 (softer pastel) / Blue-Light: #2A65F0 / Blue-Dark: #6B95FF
- `brand/primary-hover`, `brand/primary-active`, `brand/primary-subtle`, `brand/primary-muted`
- `brand/secondary` (derived: tints/shades of primary)
- `brand/on-primary` (text/icon color on brand bg)

**Semantic status:**
- `status/success/{default,subtle,muted,on}`
- `status/warning/{default,subtle,muted,on}`
- `status/error/{default,subtle,muted,on}`
- `status/info/{default,subtle,muted,on}`

**Interactive states (utilities):**
- `state/hover-overlay`, `state/active-overlay`, `state/focus-ring`, `state/disabled-opacity`

### Tier 3 — `Component/*` Collections (per-component)

Each component gets its own collection. Variables reference Tier 2 (semantic) — so a brand change cascades automatically. Naming: `{element}/{property}/{state}`.

**Example: `Component/Button` collection**
```
Primary/bg/default       → brand/primary
Primary/bg/hover         → brand/primary-hover
Primary/bg/active        → brand/primary-active
Primary/bg/disabled      → surface/sunken
Primary/text/default     → brand/on-primary
Primary/text/disabled    → text/disabled
Primary/border/focus     → border/focus

Secondary/bg/default     → surface/raised
Secondary/text/default   → text/primary
...

Outline/bg/default       → transparent
Outline/border/default   → border/strong
Outline/text/default     → text/primary
...

Ghost/bg/default         → transparent
Ghost/bg/hover           → state/hover-overlay
...

Destructive/bg/default   → status/error/default
...
```

(Full list defined when we build each component)

---

## Typography (your fonts)

**Font Variables** (`Typography` collection):
- `font/sans` = **Inter** (primary — UI, headings, body)
- `font/serif` = **Zoho Puvi** (secondary — marketing, accent headings, brand moments)
- `font/mono` = **Roboto Mono** (code blocks only)

**Type Scale** (text styles, all using `font/sans` by default):
| Style | Size | Weight | Line Height | Letter Spacing |
|-------|------|--------|-------------|----------------|
| Display/2XL | 60px | 700 | 72px | -2% |
| Display/XL | 48px | 700 | 56px | -2% |
| Display/L | 40px | 700 | 48px | -1% |
| Heading/H1 | 32px | 600 | 40px | -1% |
| Heading/H2 | 28px | 600 | 36px | -0.5% |
| Heading/H3 | 24px | 600 | 32px | 0 |
| Heading/H4 | 20px | 600 | 28px | 0 |
| Heading/H5 | 18px | 600 | 26px | 0 |
| Body/Large | 16px | 400 | 24px | 0 |
| Body/Default | 14px | 400 | 20px | 0 |
| Body/Small | 13px | 400 | 18px | 0 |
| Label/Default | 12px | 500 | 16px | +1% |
| Label/Small | 11px | 500 | 14px | +1% |
| Caption | 10px | 400 | 14px | +2% |
| Code/Default | 13px | 400 (mono) | 20px | 0 |
| Code/Small | 12px | 400 (mono) | 16px | 0 |
| Brand/Display (Puvi) | 48px | 700 | 56px | -2% |
| Brand/Heading (Puvi) | 32px | 600 | 40px | -1% |

---

## Spacing, Radius, Shadows

### Spacing (`Spacing` collection, 4px base)
`0, 1, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32, 40, 48, 56, 64, 80, 96, 128, 160`

### Radius (`Radius` collection)
`none(0), xs(2), sm(4), md(6), lg(8), xl(12), 2xl(16), 3xl(24), full(9999)`

### Shadows (`Shadows` collection — Light + Dark modes)
- `elevation/0` (none)
- `elevation/1` (subtle hover)
- `elevation/2` (cards, dropdowns)
- `elevation/3` (popovers, menus)
- `elevation/4` (modals)
- `elevation/5` (toasts, top-level)
- `focus/ring` (focus state outline)
- `glow/brand` (futuristic accent glow — uses brand color)

---

## File Structure (Figma Pages)

| # | Page | When |
|---|------|------|
| 1 | 🏠 Cover | Phase 0 |
| 2 | 📐 Foundations — Variables & Tokens | Phase 1 |
| 3 | 🔤 Typography | Phase 1 |
| 4 | 🎨 Colors | Phase 1 |
| 5 | 📏 Spacing & Layout | Phase 1 |
| 6 | ✨ Effects (Shadows, Glow) | Phase 1 |
| 7 | 🎯 Icons | Phase 2 |
| 8 | 🧱 Components — Atoms | Phase 3 |
| 9 | 🧬 Components — Molecules | Phase 4 |
| 10 | 🏛️ Components — Organisms | Phase 5 |
| 11 | 📄 Patterns | Phase 6 |
| 12 | 📝 Documentation | Ongoing |

---

## Build Order (Strict Dependency Chain — One at a Time)

You will build these in this exact order. Each item depends on the previous.

### Phase 0 — File Setup
1. Create Figma file "OM Design System"
2. Set up pages, cover, naming convention

### Phase 1 — Foundations *(must complete before any component)*
1. `_Primitives` collection — all raw color scales
2. `_Semantic` collection — 4 modes (Orange-Light, Orange-Dark, Blue-Light, Blue-Dark)
3. `Typography` collection + 18 text styles
4. `Spacing` collection
5. `Radius` collection
6. `Shadows` collection — effect styles for both modes
7. Build a **theme-test frame** with sample text/surfaces — verify all 4 modes switch cleanly

### Phase 2 — Icon System *(blocks every component)*
1. Import all 382 ZCat-Icons as components
2. Wrap into a single **Icon** master component with:
   - Property: `name` (instance swap → links to icon library)
   - Property: `size` (variant: 12, 14, 16, 20, 24, 32)
   - Property: `color` (bound to `text/primary` by default; overridable)
3. Every other component MUST use this Icon master via instance swap

### Phase 3 — Atoms (build in this order, each blocks the next)

| # | Atom | Composed From | Why this order |
|---|------|---------------|----------------|
| 1 | **Icon** | (primitive) | Foundation for all |
| 2 | **Text Label** | Typography styles | Used in everything |
| 3 | **Divider** | (primitive) | Used in Menu, Card, Modal |
| 4 | **Spinner** | (primitive) | Used in Button loading, Loading states |
| 5 | **Button** | Icon, Text Label, Spinner | Used in Modal, Toast, Table, Forms |
| 6 | **Checkbox** | Icon (check/dash) | Used in Multi-select, Table, Form |
| 7 | **Radio** | (primitive) | Used in Form |
| 8 | **Toggle/Switch** | (primitive) | Used in Settings, Form |
| 9 | **Input Field (base)** | Icon, Text Label | Used in Select, Search, Date, Form |
| 10 | **Avatar** | Icon, Text | Used in Menu, Card, Table |
| 11 | **Badge** | Text | Used in Menu, Sidebar, Tab, Card |
| 12 | **Tag/Chip** | Icon, Text | Used in Multi-select, Filter, Card |
| 13 | **Tooltip** | Text | Used in everything (hover help) |

### Phase 4 — Molecules (composed from Atoms)

| # | Molecule | Composed From |
|---|----------|---------------|
| 14 | **Menu Item** | Icon, Text, Badge, Checkbox |
| 15 | **Dropdown Menu** | Menu Item, Divider |
| 16 | **Select (single)** | Input Field, Dropdown Menu, Icon |
| 17 | **Select (multi)** | Input Field, Dropdown Menu, Checkbox, Tag |
| 18 | **Search Bar** | Input Field, Icon, Button |
| 19 | **Date Picker** | Input Field, Button, Icon, calendar grid |
| 20 | **Tabs** | Text, Icon, Badge |
| 21 | **Breadcrumb** | Text, Icon |
| 22 | **Pagination** | Button, Icon, Text |
| 23 | **Card** | Text, Button, Divider, Icon |
| 24 | **Alert** | Icon, Text, Button |
| 25 | **Toast** | Icon, Text, Button |
| 26 | **Progress (Linear/Circular)** | (primitive + text) |
| 27 | **Skeleton** | (primitive) |
| 28 | **Accordion Item** | Icon, Text, Divider |

### Phase 5 — Organisms (composed from Molecules + Atoms)

| # | Organism | Composed From |
|---|----------|---------------|
| 29 | **Modal/Dialog** | Button, Input Field, Icon, Divider |
| 30 | **Drawer** | Button, Icon, Divider, any content slots |
| 31 | **Table** | Checkbox, Button, Icon, Tag, Avatar, Pagination |
| 32 | **Sidebar Nav** | Icon, Text, Badge, Avatar, Divider |
| 33 | **Top Bar / Header** | Icon, Search Bar, Avatar, Dropdown Menu, Button |
| 34 | **Form** | All input atoms + Button |
| 35 | **Empty State** | Icon, Text, Button |
| 36 | **File Upload** | Icon, Button, Progress |

### Phase 6 — Patterns
- Login / Sign-up
- Dashboard layout
- Settings page
- Data table page (with filters + pagination)
- Empty/Error states (404, 500, no-data)

---

## Composition Rules (Enforced Per Component)

When building each component, follow this checklist:
- [ ] Auto Layout used everywhere (no absolute positioning)
- [ ] Every icon = **instance of Icon component** (instance swap property)
- [ ] Every nested control = **instance of its atom** (Button, Checkbox, etc.)
- [ ] All colors bound to **`Component/{Name}` variables** (which alias semantic tokens)
- [ ] All spacing bound to `Spacing` variables
- [ ] All radii bound to `Radius` variables
- [ ] All text uses defined text styles
- [ ] Component properties exposed for: text content, icon swap, boolean toggles (disabled/loading/etc.), variant
- [ ] States covered: Default, Hover, Active/Pressed, Focus, Disabled (+ Error/Loading where relevant)
- [ ] Tested in all 4 modes (Orange-Light, Orange-Dark, Blue-Light, Blue-Dark)
- [ ] Atomic primitives prefixed with `_` to hide from publish

---

## Guided Workflow (How We Work Together)

For each component, we'll do this loop:
1. **I describe** the component's anatomy, variants, states, and required atoms
2. **I list** the per-component variables you need to create (with semantic alias mapping)
3. **You build** in Figma; ask questions as needed
4. **I verify** structure (you share screenshot or invite me via MCP)
5. **Move to next** component

Estimated cadence: **1–2 atoms per session**, faster as patterns repeat.

---

## Decisions Made
- **Primary brand**: Orange #FD6303 (default theme)
- **Secondary brand**: Blue #2A65F0 (alt theme — full theme swap, not coexistence)
- **Dark mode primary**: Softer/pastel (#FFB066 orange, #6B95FF blue) for eye comfort
- **Neutrals**: Cool blue-grey tint
- **Accent strategy**: Derived from primary (tints/shades) — no separate violet/cyan
- **Fonts**: Inter (primary), Zoho Puvi (secondary brand), Roboto Mono (code only)
- **Icons**: 382 ZCat-Icons, wrapped into single Icon master with instance-swap
- **Architecture**: Per-component variable collections; ZCatalyst-style structure
- **Composition**: Strict atomic — no duplicated primitives anywhere

## Excluded (for now)
- Code library (Figma only)
- Charts (separate later)
- Mobile-specific components
- Animation/motion specs

---

## Verification (per phase)
- **Foundations**: Toggle all 4 modes on test frame — every surface/text/border switches cleanly
- **Icons**: Icon master swaps to any of 382 icons via single property
- **Atoms**: Each atom passes 11-point composition checklist
- **Molecules/Organisms**: Replacing the source atom (e.g., changing Button padding) propagates to ALL parent components — no broken instances
- **Final**: Publish library → enable in test file → all components render in both themes
"}

data.reasoningText: I need to revise my approach to use Inter as the primary font, Zoho Puvi as secondary, and Roboto Mono for code, then build this fresh without referencing the old design. The key is working component by component with atomic composition—pulling icons from an Icon component, checkboxes from a Checkbox component, and so on, so that any updates cascade through nested instances automatically.

data.content:
Here's the refined plan:

---

## Plan: OM Design System (Figma) — Fresh, Atomic, Guided

Fresh Figma design system "OM Design System" with **per-component color variables** (ZCatalyst-style structure), **strict atomic composition** (every component reuses other components — never duplicates), and **theme switching** between Orange #FD6303 and Blue #2A65F0, each with Light/Dark modes.

---

### Core Principles (Non-Negotiable)

1. **Atomic Composition** — Higher components are built from instances of lower components.
   - Multi-select dropdown's checkbox = **instance of Checkbox component**
   - Modal's button = **instance of Button component**
   - Table's button = **instance of Button component**
   - Change Button padding once → propagates everywhere automatically
2. **Icons ONLY from Icon component** — no raw SVGs anywhere
3. **Per-component variables** — each component owns its color tokens
4. **Variable-bound everything** — no hard-coded hex

---

### Variable Architecture

**Collections:**
- **`_Primitives`** — Raw color scales (Orange, Blue, Cool Neutral, Red, Green, Amber, Sky)
- **`_Semantic`** with **4 modes**: `Orange-Light`, `Orange-Dark`, `Blue-Light`, `Blue-Dark`
  - Tokens: `surface/*`, `text/*`, `border/*`, `brand/*`, `status/*`, `state/*`
- **`Component/{Name}`** — Each component has its own collection, aliases semantic tokens
  - e.g., `Component/Button → Primary/bg/default → brand/primary`
- **`Typography`**, **`Spacing`**, **`Radius`**, **`Shadows`**

**Theme Modes:**
| Mode | brand/primary |
|------|---------------|
| Orange-Light | `#FD6303` |
| Orange-Dark | `#FFB066` (softer pastel) |
| Blue-Light | `#2A65F0` |
| Blue-Dark | `#6B95FF` (softer pastel) |

Neutrals: cool blue-grey tinted. Accent: derived from primary (tints/shades).

---

### Typography

| Variable | Font | Use |
|----------|------|-----|
| `font/sans` | **Inter** | Primary — UI, body, headings |
| `font/serif` | **Zoho Puvi** | Secondary — brand moments, marketing headings |
| `font/mono` | **Roboto Mono** | Code blocks only |

**18 text styles**: Display 2XL→L, Heading H1→H5, Body L/D/S, Label D/S, Caption, Code D/S, Brand Display/Heading (Puvi).

---

### Build Order (One at a Time)

**Phase 0** — File setup, pages, naming

**Phase 1 — Foundations** (must finish first)
1. `_Primitives` 2. `_Semantic` (4 modes) 3. Typography + 18 styles 4. Spacing 5. Radius 6. Shadows 7. Theme-test frame to verify 4-mode switch

**Phase 2 — Icon System** (blocks every component)
- Import 382 ZCat-Icons → wrap in single **Icon master** with `name` (instance swap), `size` (12/14/16/20/24/32), `color`
- Every future component uses Icon via instance swap

**Phase 3 — Atoms** (each blocks the next)

| # | Atom | Composed From |
|---|------|---------------|
| 1 | Icon | (primitive) |
| 2 | Text Label | Type styles |
| 3 | Divider | (primitive) |
| 4 | Spinner | (primitive) |
| 5 | **Button** | Icon, Text, Spinner |
| 6 | **Checkbox** | Icon |
| 7 | Radio | (primitive) |
| 8 | Toggle/Switch | (primitive) |
| 9 | **Input Field** | Icon, Text |
| 10 | Avatar | Icon, Text |
| 11 | Badge | Text |
| 12 | Tag/Chip | Icon, Text |
| 13 | Tooltip | Text |

**Phase 4 — Molecules**
14 Menu Item · 15 Dropdown Menu · 16 Select (single) · 17 Select (multi) · 18 Search Bar · 19 Date Picker · 20 Tabs · 21 Breadcrumb · 22 Pagination · 23 Card · 24 Alert · 25 Toast · 26 Progress · 27 Skeleton · 28 Accordion

**Phase 5 — Organisms**
29 Modal · 30 Drawer · 31 Table · 32 Sidebar Nav · 33 Top Bar · 34 Form · 35 Empty State · 36 File Upload

**Phase 6 — Patterns**
Login, Dashboard, Settings, Data Table page, Empty/Error states.

---

### Per-Component Composition Checklist
- [ ] Auto Layout everywhere
- [ ] Every icon = Icon component instance (swap property)
- [ ] Every nested control = atom instance
- [ ] Colors bound to `Component/{Name}` variables (alias semantic)
- [ ] Spacing/radius bound to variables
- [ ] Text uses defined styles
- [ ] Properties exposed (text, icon swap, booleans, variant)
- [ ] States: Default · Hover · Active · Focus · Disabled (+ Error/Loading)
- [ ] Tested in all 4 modes
- [ ] Internal atoms prefixed `_` (hidden from publish)

---

### Our Workflow (per component)

1. **I describe** anatomy, variants, states, required atoms
2. **I list** the per-component variables to create + semantic mapping
3. **You build** in Figma, ask questions
4. **I verify** (screenshot or via MCP)
5. **Move to next**

---

### Decisions
- **Brands**: Orange #FD6303 + Blue #2A65F0 (full theme swap, not coexistence)
- **Dark primary**: softer pastel
- **Neutrals**: cool blue-grey
- **Accent**: derived from primary
- **Fonts**: Inter + Zoho Puvi + Roboto Mono
- **Icons**: 382 ZCat-Icons in single Icon master
- **Variables**: ZCatalyst-style per-component structure

### Excluded

### Verification

- Icons: master swaps to any of 382

font is like if i switch font family in global like from inter to puvi measn all component should be in puvi?

Yes — exactly. That's the power of using **font variables** instead of hard-coded font names. Here's how it works:

---

### How Global Font Swap Works

**Setup (one time):**
- Create a Figma variable `font/sans` = `"Inter"` (string variable)
- Every text style (`Body/Default`, `Heading/H1`, etc.) binds its **Font Family** field to `font/sans` — not typed as "Inter"
- Every component uses these **text styles** (not raw font settings)

**Result:**
- Change `font/sans` from `"Inter"` → `"Zoho Puvi"` in one place
- → All 18 text styles instantly become Puvi
- → Every Button, Input, Modal, Table, etc. that uses those styles → Puvi
- → Across the entire design system, in one click

---

### Recommended Setup (3 Font Variables, Decoupled)

| Variable | Default | Purpose |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `font/sans` | Inter | UI font — drives 90% of components |
| `font/serif` | Zoho Puvi | Brand/marketing accents (only where you explicitly want it) |
| `font/mono` | Roboto Mono | Code blocks only |

**Why decoupled?** If you want Puvi *everywhere*, just point `font/sans` → `"Zoho Puvi"`. Done. Or keep them separate for mixed use (UI in Inter, hero headings in Puvi).

---

### Even Better — Theme-Mode Font Switching

You can make font part of the **mode itself**. Example:

Add a new collection `Brand` with modes:
- `Mode: Inter Brand` → `font/sans = "Inter"`
- `Mode: Puvi Brand` → `font/sans = "Zoho Puvi"`

Then switching the brand mode on any frame → swaps fonts globally for that frame's children, no manual edits.

---

### Critical Rule

**For this to work, EVERY text layer must use a defined text style** — never typed-in font settings. If someone overrides font directly on a layer, the variable swap won't reach it.

This is added to the per-component checklist as: *"All text uses defined text styles (no manual font overrides)"*.

Want me to update the plan to add the font-mode collection, or keep it as 3 simple variables for now?

i have figma professional plan, 4 modes not possible?

Good catch — let me clarify the Figma plan limits and propose a smarter setup.

---

### Figma Mode Limits per Plan
| Plan | Modes per collection |
|------|---------------------|
| Starter (Free) | 1 |
| **Professional** | **4** |
| Organization | unlimited |
| Enterprise | unlimited |

So **4 modes IS possible** on Professional — but you'd be hitting the cap with no room to grow (e.g., adding a high-contrast accessibility mode later would break it).

---

### Better Approach: Split into 2 Collections (2 modes each)

Instead of one collection with 4 combined modes, split the dimensions:

**Collection 1: `_Theme`** (2 modes)
- `Orange` 
- `Blue`
- Holds only the **brand color tokens** that change between themes
- Tokens: `brand/primary`, `brand/primary-hover`, `brand/primary-active`, `brand/primary-subtle`, `brand/on-primary`, `brand/secondary`, `state/focus-ring`

**Collection 2: `_Appearance`** (2 modes)
- `Light`
- `Dark`
- Holds everything else that changes with light/dark
- Tokens: `surface/*`, `text/*`, `border/*`, `status/*`, `state/hover-overlay`, `state/active-overlay`, shadows

**How they combine:**
- A frame can have **both** modes set independently: `_Theme = Orange` + `_Appearance = Dark`
- Component variables alias both as needed (Button/bg/default → `brand/primary`; Card/bg → `surface/card`)
- Switching either mode on a frame instantly cascades

---

### Why This Is Better

| Aspect | 4-mode single collection | 2 split collections |
|--------|-------------------------|---------------------|
| Modes used | 4/4 (maxed out) | 2/4 each (room to grow) |
| Add high-contrast later | ❌ blocked | ✅ add to `_Appearance` |
| Add a 3rd brand later (e.g., Green) | ❌ blocked | ✅ add to `_Theme` |
| Maintain duplicate tokens | ❌ Light values typed twice (Orange+Blue) | ✅ Light surface defined once |
| Switch only theme without touching dark/light | ❌ needs a different mode | ✅ independent toggle |
| Total variables to manage | ~2x duplication | ~50% fewer |

The split approach is cleaner, scales further, and saves you from typing the same `surface/card` value across Orange-Light + Blue-Light.

---

### Updated Mode Budget

| Collection | Modes | Used / Limit |
|-----------|-------|--------------|
| `_Primitives` | none | — |
| `_Theme` | Orange, Blue | 2/4 |
| `_Appearance` | Light, Dark | 2/4 |
| `Typography` | none | — |
| `Spacing` | none | — |
| `Radius` | none | — |
| `Shadows` | Light, Dark (alias `_Appearance`) | 2/4 |
| `Component/*` | none (resolves through aliases) | — |

Plenty of headroom on every collection.

---

Want me to update the plan to use this split-collection approach? It's a small change but makes the foundation much more scalable.

---

## Final Variable Architecture

### Two Independent Mode Toggles

**Collection 1: `_Appearance`** → 2 modes: **Light, Dark**
- Holds everything that changes between light/dark surfaces
- Tokens: `surface/*`, `text/*`, `border/*`, `status/*`, `state/hover-overlay`, `state/active-overlay`

**Collection 2: `_Theme`** → 2 modes: **Orange, Blue**
- Holds only the brand color tokens
- Tokens: `brand/primary`, `brand/primary-hover`, `brand/primary-active`, `brand/primary-subtle`, `brand/primary-muted`, `brand/on-primary`, `brand/secondary`, `state/focus-ring`

### How They Combine (per frame)

You set both toggles independently on any frame → 4 effective combinations:

| Appearance | Theme | brand/primary | surface/base |
|-----------|-------|---------------|--------------|
| Light | Orange | `#FD6303` | white |
| Dark | Orange | `#FFB066` (pastel) | dark navy |
| Light | Blue | `#2A65F0` | white |
| Dark | Blue | `#6B95FF` (pastel) | dark navy |

### Why This Is Better Than One 4-Mode Collection
- **Mode budget**: 2/4 used per collection → room for high-contrast, density, or 3rd brand later
- **Less duplication**: `surface/card` value typed once, not 2× (Orange-Light + Blue-Light)
- **Independent switching**: change just the theme without touching dark/light, or vice versa
- **Cleaner mental model**: appearance ≠ brand

### Full Mode Budget (Figma Pro = 4/collection cap)

| Collection | Modes | Used |
|------------|-------|------|
| `_Primitives` | — | 0/4 |
| `_Appearance` | Light, Dark | 2/4 |
| `_Theme` | Orange, Blue | 2/4 |
| `Shadows` | Light, Dark | 2/4 |
| `Typography` / `Spacing` / `Radius` / `Component/*` | — | 0/4 |

Plenty of headroom everywhere.

---

Plan updated. Here's the new lean approach:

---

## Updated Phase 1 — Minimal Foundations

We create only the **structure** + **bare-minimum tokens**, then add colors per-component as we go.

### Step 1.1 — Create Empty Collection Shells
- `_Primitives` (no modes)
- `_Appearance` (Light, Dark)
- `_Theme` (Orange, Blue)
- `Typography`, `Spacing`, `Radius` (no modes)
- `Shadows` (Light, Dark)
- `Component/*` collections → **created on-demand per component**

### Step 1.2 — Seed Only Bare Minimum
| Collection | What to add now | What to skip |
|------------|-----------------|--------------|
| `_Primitives` | Neutral grey scale (50–950) + white/black/transparent | Orange, Blue, Red, Green, Amber, Sky scales |
| `_Appearance` | `surface/base`, `surface/card`, `text/primary`, `text/secondary`, `border/default` | Everything else |
| `_Theme` | **Nothing yet** — first brand token added with Spinner | All brand tokens |
| `Typography` | 3 font vars (Inter, Puvi, Roboto Mono) + 6 styles (H1, H2, Body/D, Body/S, Label/D, Code/D) | 12 other styles |
| `Spacing` | Full scale (it's cheap) | — |
| `Radius` | Full scale (it's cheap) | — |
| `Shadows` | **Skip until first elevated component** | All |

### Step 1.3 — Two Test Frames
- Light frame + Dark frame with sample text/cards → verify Appearance modes switch

That's all of Phase 1. **No brand colors, no per-component variables yet.**

---

## Updated Phase 3 — Per-Component Token Loop

For **each atom** we build, the workflow becomes:

1. **I list** the new tokens needed (added to `_Primitives` / `_Appearance` / `_Theme` + new `Component/{Name}` collection)
2. **You add** those tokens
3. **You build** the component using them
4. **We verify** in Light/Dark × Orange/Blue
5. **Move on**

### When Each Token Arrives

| Component | First introduces |
|-----------|-----------------|
| **Icon** | `Component/Icon` collection (icon colors) |
| **Text Label** | text/tertiary, text/disabled, text/inverse, text/on-brand |
| **Divider** | border/subtle |
| **Spinner** | 🎯 First brand tokens — Orange + Blue scales in `_Primitives`, full `brand/*` in `_Theme` |
| **Button** | State overlays, focus ring, full button per-component vars |
| **Checkbox** | Checkbox per-component vars |
| **Input** | status/error, status/success |
| **Badge** | Full status/* set (success/warning/error/info) + their primitive scales |
| **Tooltip** | surface/inverse |
| **Card** | First shadow tokens added |

---

### Benefits of This Approach
- **Zero waste** — only create tokens you actually use
- **Faster start** — Phase 1 becomes ~30 mins instead of half a day
- **Cleaner library** — no unused variables cluttering the panel
- **Just-in-time learning** — you learn each token in context of where it's used

---

data.content:
Here is the **final plan**:

---

# OM Design System (Figma) — Final Plan

## TL;DR
Fresh Figma design system built **one component at a time**, with **per-component color variables** (ZCatalyst-style), **strict atomic composition** (every component reuses other components — no duplication), and **independent dual-mode toggling**: Light/Dark × Orange/Blue. Tokens are added incrementally, only when a component needs them.

---

## Core Principles (Non-Negotiable)

1. **Atomic Composition** — Higher components are built from instances of lower components. Change Button padding once → every Modal/Table/Form using it updates automatically.
2. **Icons ONLY from Icon component** — no raw SVGs anywhere
3. **Per-component color variables** — each component owns its tokens
4. **Variable-bound everything** — no hard-coded hex
5. **Just-in-time tokens** — only create what you need, when you need it

---

## Variable Architecture (Split-Mode for Figma Pro)

| Collection | Modes | Holds |
|------------|-------|-------|
| `_Primitives` | — | Raw color scales (added per-need: neutrals first; brand/status added later) |
| `_Appearance` | **Light, Dark** | `surface/*`, `text/*`, `border/*`, `status/*`, `state/hover-overlay`, `state/active-overlay` |
| `_Theme` | **Orange, Blue** | `brand/primary`, `brand/primary-hover/active/subtle/muted`, `brand/on-primary`, `brand/secondary`, `state/focus-ring` |
| `Typography` | — | `font/sans`, `font/serif`, `font/mono` |
| `Spacing` | — | 4px-base scale |
| `Radius` | — | none → full |
| `Shadows` | **Light, Dark** | elevations, focus ring, brand glow |
| `Component/{Name}` | — | Per-component aliases (one collection per component, created on-demand) |

**Frame-level toggle**: each frame independently sets `_Appearance` and `_Theme` → 4 effective combinations (Light+Orange, Dark+Orange, Light+Blue, Dark+Blue).

**Mode budget**: 2/4 used per collection — room for high-contrast, density, or 3rd brand later.

---

## Theme Color Anchors

| Combination | brand/primary |
|-------------|---------------|
| Light + Orange | `#FD6303` |
| Dark + Orange | `#FFB066` (softer pastel) |
| Light + Blue | `#2A65F0` |
| Dark + Blue | `#6B95FF` (softer pastel) |

- **Neutrals**: cool blue-grey tinted
- **Accent**: derived from primary (tints/shades)

---

## Typography

| Variable | Font | Use |
|----------|------|-----|
| `font/sans` | **Inter** | UI, body, headings (90% of system) |
| `font/serif` | **Zoho Puvi** | Brand/marketing accents |
| `font/mono` | **Roboto Mono** | Code blocks only |

- Text styles include **line-height as part of the style** — no separate line-height tokens
- Components use text styles; line-height comes with them
- Override line-height inside a component only when layout truly requires it (e.g., dense table rows)
- **Global font swap** works automatically: change `font/sans` → all components update

---

## Spacing / Radius / Shadows

- **Spacing** (4px base): `0, 1, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32, 40, 48, 56, 64, 80, 96, 128, 160`
- **Radius**: `none(0), xs(2), sm(4), md(6), lg(8), xl(12), 2xl(16), 3xl(24), full(9999)`
- **Shadows** (Light/Dark): `elevation/0–5`, `focus/ring`, `glow/brand` (added when first elevated component is built)

---

## Build Order

### Phase 0 — File Setup
Create file, pages (Cover, Foundations, Typography, Colors, Spacing, Effects, Icons, Atoms, Molecules, Organisms, Patterns, Docs), naming convention.

### Phase 1 — Minimal Foundations *(lean — only essentials)*

**1.1 Create empty collection shells** (all 7 collections — see table above)

**1.2 Seed only bare-minimum tokens**

| Collection | Add now | Skip until needed |
|------------|---------|-------------------|
| `_Primitives` | Neutral grey 50–950 + white/black/transparent | Orange, Blue, Red, Green, Amber, Sky scales |
| `_Appearance` | `surface/base`, `surface/card`, `text/primary`, `text/secondary`, `border/default` | All other tokens |
| `_Theme` | **Nothing** | All brand tokens (added with Spinner) |
| `Typography` | 3 font vars + 6 styles (H1, H2, Body/D, Body/S, Label/D, Code/D) | 12 other styles |
| `Spacing` | Full scale | — |
| `Radius` | Full scale | — |
| `Shadows` | **Nothing** | All (added with first elevated component) |

**1.3 Verify** — Build 2 test frames (Light, Dark) with sample text/cards → confirm Appearance mode switches cleanly.

### Phase 2 — Icon System *(blocks every component)*
- Import 382 ZCat-Icons as components
- Wrap into single **Icon master** with properties: `name` (instance swap), `size` (12/14/16/20/24/32), `color`
- Create `Component/Icon` collection: `icon/color/default → text/primary`, `icon/color/muted → text/secondary` (more added on-demand)

### Phase 3 — Atoms *(per-component loop)*

For each atom: **(1)** I list new tokens needed → **(2)** you add them → **(3)** you build → **(4)** verify in Light/Dark × Orange/Blue → **(5)** next.

| # | Atom | First introduces |
|---|------|-----------------|
| 1 | Icon | `Component/Icon` collection |
| 2 | Text Label | `text/tertiary, text/disabled, text/inverse, text/on-brand` |
| 3 | Divider | `border/subtle` |
| 4 | Spinner | 🎯 First brand tokens — Orange + Blue scales in `_Primitives`, full `brand/*` in `_Theme` |
| 5 | Button | State overlays, focus ring, full Button per-component vars |
| 6 | Checkbox | Checkbox per-component vars |
| 7 | Radio | Radio per-component vars |
| 8 | Toggle/Switch | Toggle per-component vars |
| 9 | Input Field | `status/error`, `status/success` + Input per-component vars |
| 10 | Avatar | Avatar per-component vars |
| 11 | Badge | Full `status/*` set + status primitive scales |
| 12 | Tag/Chip | Tag per-component vars |
| 13 | Tooltip | `surface/inverse` + Tooltip per-component vars |

### Phase 4 — Molecules
14 Menu Item · 15 Dropdown Menu · 16 Select (single) · 17 Select (multi) · 18 Search Bar · 19 Date Picker · 20 Tabs · 21 Breadcrumb · 22 Pagination · 23 Card *(introduces shadow tokens)* · 24 Alert · 25 Toast · 26 Progress · 27 Skeleton · 28 Accordion

### Phase 5 — Organisms
29 Modal · 30 Drawer · 31 Table · 32 Sidebar Nav · 33 Top Bar · 34 Form · 35 Empty State · 36 File Upload

### Phase 6 — Patterns
Login · Dashboard · Settings · Data Table page · Empty/Error states (404, 500, no-data)

---

## Per-Component Composition Checklist
- [ ] Auto Layout everywhere
- [ ] Every icon = Icon component instance (swap property)
- [ ] Every nested control = atom instance (no duplicating Buttons inside Modals, etc.)
- [ ] Colors bound to `Component/{Name}` variables (which alias `_Appearance`/`_Theme`)
- [ ] Spacing/radius bound to variables
- [ ] All text uses defined text styles (no manual font overrides — keeps global font swap working)
- [ ] Properties exposed: text content, icon swap, booleans (disabled/loading/etc.), variant
- [ ] States: Default · Hover · Active · Focus · Disabled (+ Error/Loading where relevant)
- [ ] Tested in Light/Dark × Orange/Blue
- [ ] Internal atoms prefixed `_` (hidden from publish)

---

## Workflow Loop (per component)
1. **I describe** anatomy, variants, states, required atoms
2. **I list** the new tokens to create + semantic mapping
3. **You build** in Figma; ask questions
4. **I verify** (screenshot or via Figma MCP)
5. **Move to next**

---

## Decisions
- Brands: Orange #FD6303 + Blue #2A65F0 (full theme swap, not coexistence)
- Dark primary: softer pastel for eye comfort
- Neutrals: cool blue-grey
- Accent: derived from primary
- Fonts: Inter + Zoho Puvi + Roboto Mono
- Icons: 382 ZCat-Icons in single Icon master
- Variables: ZCatalyst-style per-component, split modes (Appearance × Theme)
- Tokens added incrementally per component — no upfront waste

## Excluded
Code library · Charts · Mobile-native components · Motion specs

## Verification
- **Foundations**: Light/Dark switch on test frame works
- **Icons**: Master swaps to any of 382
- **Atoms**: Pass 11-point checklist
- **Composition test**: Edit Button padding once → propagates to Modal, Table, Form (proves architecture)
- **Final**: Publish library → enable in fresh test file → all components render in both Appearance × both Theme modes

---

**Plan saved.** Ready to start **Phase 0 (file setup) + Phase 1.1 (collection shells)** whenever you say go — I'll give you exact step-by-step instructions with the seed values.
all component colors shoud come from premitive colors hex code right?

Yes — exactly right. Every actual color value lives in `_Primitives` as raw hex. Everything else just *references* through aliases. Here's the chain:

---

## The Reference Chain (Hex → Component)

```
_Primitives (hex values)
        ↓ aliased by
_Appearance / _Theme (semantic)
        ↓ aliased by
Component/{Name} (per-component)
        ↓ used in
Component layer (fill, stroke, text)
```

**Only `_Primitives` contains hex values.** Every other variable is an alias pointing to another variable. No layer in any component should ever reference a primitive directly — always go through the chain.

---

## Concrete Example: Primary Button Background

**Step 1 — `_Primitives`** (raw hex, the only place hex exists):
```
orange/600 = #FD6303
orange/400 = #FFB066
blue/600   = #2A65F0
blue/400   = #6B95FF
```

**Step 2 — `_Theme`** (alias by mode):
```
brand/primary  → Orange mode: orange/600   Blue mode: blue/600
                 (in Dark Appearance, Theme mode could resolve via separate logic, 
                  but typically: brand/primary stays the same in _Theme; 
                  the Appearance variant for dark uses different shades)
```

Wait — clarification needed here. Let me show the cleanest pattern:

**Better pattern** — `_Theme` holds the brand swap, `_Appearance` holds the dark adjustment via a separate set:

```
_Theme (Orange mode):
  brand/primary       → orange/600   (#FD6303)
  brand/primary-dark  → orange/400   (#FFB066)

_Theme (Blue mode):
  brand/primary       → blue/600     (#2A65F0)
  brand/primary-dark  → blue/400     (#6B95FF)
```

Then `Component/Button` decides which to use based on Appearance:
```
Component/Button:
  Primary/bg/default → brand/primary       (used in Light Appearance)
```

…but wait, this doesn't auto-switch with Appearance. **The cleanest solution** is to put the appearance-aware brand selection inside `_Appearance`:

**Final clean pattern:**

```
_Theme (Orange):
  brand/scale-strong → orange/600   (#FD6303)
  brand/scale-soft   → orange/400   (#FFB066)

_Theme (Blue):
  brand/scale-strong → blue/600     (#2A65F0)
  brand/scale-soft   → blue/400     (#6B95FF)

_Appearance (Light):
  brand/primary → brand/scale-strong   (uses #FD6303 or #2A65F0 depending on theme)

_Appearance (Dark):
  brand/primary → brand/scale-soft     (uses #FFB066 or #6B95FF depending on theme)

Component/Button:
  Primary/bg/default → brand/primary   (resolves to correct hex automatically)
```

---

## Why This Matters

- ✅ Change `#FD6303` → new orange? **Edit one place** (`_Primitives.orange/600`) → cascades to every Orange-mode component
- ✅ Decide softer dark mode? **Edit one place** (swap `_Appearance.Dark.brand/primary` from `scale-soft` to `scale-medium`)
- ✅ Add a new brand (Green)? **Add one mode** to `_Theme` → instantly works everywhere
- ✅ Component devs never touch hex — they only work with `Component/Button.Primary/bg/default`

---

## Rule of Thumb

| Layer | Contains |
|-------|----------|
| `_Primitives` | **Hex values only** (raw paint) |
| `_Appearance` / `_Theme` | **Aliases to `_Primitives`** (semantic meaning) |
| `Component/{Name}` | **Aliases to `_Appearance` / `_Theme`** (component-specific naming) |
| Component layers | **Aliases to `Component/{Name}`** (never to anything else) |

So yes — every visible color in every component traces back to a single hex in `_Primitives`. Want to lock this in and start building Phase 1?

toolRequests.name: memory
toolRequests.arguments:
{"command": "create", "path": "/memories/design-system-tokens.md", "file_text": "# Design System — Token Architecture (Figma Variables)

## Reference Chain (only `_Primitives` holds hex)
```
_Primitives (raw hex)  →  _Appearance / _Theme (semantic alias)  →  Component/{Name} (per-component alias)  →  Component layers
```
Component layers MUST alias `Component/{Name}` only — never `_Primitives` or semantic directly.

## Split-Mode Pattern (Figma Pro = 4 modes/collection)
- `_Primitives` (no modes): raw color scales
- `_Appearance` (Light, Dark): `surface/*`, `text/*`, `border/*`, `status/*`, hover/active overlays
- `_Theme` (Orange, Blue, ...): `brand/*`, `state/focus-ring`
- Frame independently sets _Appearance + _Theme → N×M effective combinations
- Avoids 4-mode-combined-collection (which maxes Pro plan, doubles duplication)

## Brand × Appearance Auto-Switch Pattern
Put brand SCALES in _Theme; let _Appearance pick which scale:
```
_Theme (Orange):  brand/scale-strong → orange/600    brand/scale-soft → orange/400
_Theme (Blue):    brand/scale-strong → blue/600      brand/scale-soft → blue/400
_Appearance (Light):  brand/primary → brand/scale-strong
_Appearance (Dark):   brand/primary → brand/scale-soft
Component/Button: Primary/bg/default → brand/primary  (resolves correctly per Appearance×Theme)
```

## Per-Component Variable Collections (ZCatalyst-style)
Each component owns a `Component/{Name}` collection. Naming: `{element}/{property}/{state}`.
Example Component/Button: `Primary/bg/default`, `Primary/bg/hover`, `Primary/text/default`, `Outline/border/default`, etc.
Why: per-component tweaks don't ripple unintentionally; clear ownership.

## Atomic Composition Rule (non-negotiable)
- Every higher component uses INSTANCES of lower components (Modal's button = Button instance, Multi-select's checkbox = Checkbox instance, Table's button = Button instance)
- Every icon = instance of single Icon master (with name/size/color properties)
- Edit source atom once → all parents update automatically
- Internal-only atoms prefixed `_` to hide from publish

## Typography Variables
- `font/sans`, `font/serif`, `font/mono` as STRING variables
- All text styles bind Font Family field to these variables (not typed names)
- Line-height baked INTO the text style (no separate line-height tokens); override per-component only when layout demands
- Result: change `font/sans` value once → entire system swaps font instantly
- Rule: every text layer must use a defined text style; manual font overrides break the swap

## Just-In-Time Token Creation
Don't pre-create all tokens. Add to _Primitives / _Appearance / _Theme only when a component needs them. Keeps library clean and reduces upfront work.

## Figma Plan Mode Limits
| Plan | Modes per collection |
|------|---------------------|
| Starter | 1 |
| Professional | 4 |
| Organization / Enterprise | unlimited |
"}

data.content:
Saved. Shadow architecture follows the same 3-tier chain as colors:

---

### Shadow Token Chain

**`_Primitives`** (raw values — only place hex/numbers exist):
- **Colors**: `shadow/color/black-soft = rgba(0,0,0,0.08)`, `black-med`, `black-hard`, `brand-glow`
- **Numbers**: `shadow/blur/sm|md|lg`, `shadow/y/sm|md|lg`, `shadow/spread/0|-2`

**`_Appearance`** (Light/Dark picks the right shadow color):
```
elevation/card/color  →  Light: black-soft   Dark: black-hard
```

**`Component/{Name}`** (per-component, owns its shadow):
```
Card/shadow/color   → elevation/card/color
Card/shadow/y       → shadow/y/md
Card/shadow/blur    → shadow/blur/md
Card/shadow/spread  → shadow/spread/-2
```

**Figma Effect Style** binds each numeric field + color to these variables.

---

### Why Both Color AND Numbers as Variables
- Edit Card blur from 12 → 16 once → every Card updates
- Edit `black-soft` opacity from 0.08 → 0.10 → all Light-mode shadows soften everywhere
- Modal, Dropdown, Tooltip, Popover, Toast each own their own `Component/*` shadow tokens (aliasing shared `_Appearance.elevation/*` semantics) — so tweaking Modal shadow doesn't affect Card shadow

---

Same just-in-time rule applies: shadow primitives created when first elevated component (likely **Card**) is built. Ready when you are.
