{"command": "str_replace", "new_str": "## Just-In-Time Token Creation
Don't pre-create all tokens. Add to _Primitives / _Appearance / _Theme only when a component needs them. Keeps library clean and reduces upfront work.

## Motion / Interaction Variables (same 3-tier pattern)
Every component ships with motion baked in (hover, click, focus, open/close, expand/collapse). Motion uses variables so the entire system can be slowed/sped/disabled in one place.

```
_Primitives (Number variables):
  duration/instant = 0ms
  duration/fast    = 100ms     (hovers, focus)
  duration/base    = 200ms     (clicks, toggles, default)
  duration/slow    = 300ms     (dropdowns, tooltips, accordions)
  duration/slower  = 400ms     (modals, drawers)
  duration/slowest = 600ms     (page transitions)

_Primitives (String variables for easings):
  ease/linear     = "linear"
  ease/standard   = "cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1)"      (default in/out)
  ease/decelerate = "cubic-bezier(0, 0, 0, 1)"        (entering)
  ease/accelerate = "cubic-bezier(0.3, 0, 1, 1)"      (exiting)
  ease/spring     = "cubic-bezier(0.5, 1.5, 0.5, 1)"  (playful)

Component/Button:
  Button/motion/hover/duration  → duration/fast
  Button/motion/hover/easing    → ease/standard
  Button/motion/press/duration  → duration/instant   (snappy click feedback)
  Button/motion/press/scale     → 0.97               (slight press depression)
```

## Figma Implementation of Motion
Figma supports motion via:
- **Smart Animate** in prototyping (auto-tween between variants)
- **Interactive Components** (variant change on Hover/Click/Press/Mouse Down/Mouse Up)
- **Reactions** with timing functions (Linear, Ease In/Out/Custom Bezier)

For each component:
1. Build all states as variants (Default, Hover, Active/Pressed, Focus, Disabled, Open, Closed, Expanded, Collapsed, Loading)
2. Wire interactive component reactions:
   - `On Hover → Change to Hover` (using Button/motion/hover values)
   - `On Press → Change to Active`
   - `Mouse Up → Change to Default`
   - `On Click → Open` (for dropdowns/modals/accordions)
   - `On Click outside → Close`
3. Set Smart Animate with duration + easing variables

## Standard Motion Catalog (per component type)
- **Button**: hover (color fade, fast), press (scale 0.97, instant), focus ring (fade in, fast)
- **Input/Select**: focus border (color fade + glow, base), error shake (optional)
- **Checkbox/Radio/Toggle**: check-in animation (scale+fade, base), thumb slide for toggle (base, ease/spring)
- **Dropdown/Menu**: open (slide down + fade, slow, ease/decelerate), close (fade, fast, ease/accelerate)
- **Tooltip**: appear after delay (fade + slight slide, fast)
- **Modal**: backdrop fade + content scale-from-0.95 (slower, ease/decelerate); reverse on close
- **Drawer**: slide from edge (slower, ease/decelerate)
- **Toast**: slide in from edge + fade (base, ease/decelerate); auto-dismiss with fade-out
- **Tabs**: indicator slide (base, ease/standard)
- **Accordion**: height expand + content fade (slow, ease/standard)
- **Table row**: bg color fade on hover (fast); selected highlight (instant)
- **Skeleton**: shimmer loop (1500ms, linear, infinite)
- **Spinner**: rotation loop (1000ms, linear, infinite)
- **Dialog/Popover**: scale+fade-in from origin (slow, ease/decelerate)

## Per-Component Checklist Addition (Motion)
- [ ] All state variants exist (Default/Hover/Active/Focus/Disabled + Open/Closed where applicable)
- [ ] Interactive component reactions wired (Hover, Click, Press, Mouse Up)
- [ ] Smart Animate enabled with duration + easing from Component/{Name}/motion/* variables
- [ ] Open/Close transitions use opposite easings (decelerate-in, accelerate-out)
- [ ] Loading/loop animations (spinner, skeleton) defined where applicable", "old_str": "## Just-In-Time Token Creation
Don't pre-create all tokens. Add to _Primitives / _Appearance / _Theme only when a component needs them. Keeps library clean and reduces upfront work.", "path": "/memories/design-system-tokens.md"}

data.reasoningText: The user is asking for motion and interaction patterns to be included for every component, so I need to add that to the overall plan and keep it in mind going forward.

data.content:
Saved. Here's the motion/interaction architecture:

---

## Motion Token Chain (same 3-tier pattern as colors/shadows)

### `_Primitives` — Number variables (durations) + String variables (easings)
```
duration/instant  = 0ms
duration/fast     = 100ms    (hovers, focus)
duration/base     = 200ms    (clicks, toggles, default)
duration/slow     = 300ms    (dropdowns, tooltips, accordions)
duration/slower   = 400ms    (modals, drawers)
duration/slowest  = 600ms    (page transitions)

ease/standard    = cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1)    (default)
ease/decelerate  = cubic-bezier(0, 0, 0, 1)      (entering — open/appear)
ease/accelerate  = cubic-bezier(0.3, 0, 1, 1)    (exiting — close/dismiss)
ease/spring      = cubic-bezier(0.5, 1.5, 0.5, 1)(playful — toggle thumb)
ease/linear      = linear                        (loops — spinner, shimmer)
```

### `Component/{Name}` (per-component motion tokens)
```
Button/motion/hover/duration  → duration/fast
Button/motion/hover/easing    → ease/standard
Button/motion/press/duration  → duration/instant
Button/motion/press/scale     → 0.97
```

---

## How Motion Works in Figma

1. **Build all states as variants**: Default · Hover · Active · Focus · Disabled · Open · Closed · Expanded · Collapsed · Loading
2. **Wire Interactive Component reactions**:
   - `On Hover → Hover variant` (uses `motion/hover`)
   - `On Press → Active variant` (uses `motion/press`)
   - `Mouse Up → Default`
   - `On Click → Open` (dropdowns/modals/accordions)
   - `On Click outside → Close`
3. **Enable Smart Animate** — duration + easing pulled from `Component/{Name}/motion/*` variables

---

## Standard Motion Catalog (built into each component)

| Component | Interactions |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Button** | hover (color fade, fast) · press (scale 0.97, instant) · focus ring (fade, fast) |
| **Input/Select** | focus border (color + glow, base) · error shake (optional) |
| **Checkbox/Radio** | check-in animation (scale+fade, base) |
| **Toggle** | thumb slide (base, ease/spring) |
| **Dropdown/Menu** | open: slide+fade (slow, decelerate) · close: fade (fast, accelerate) |
| **Tooltip** | appear after delay (fade+slide, fast) |
| **Modal** | backdrop fade + content scale 0.95→1 (slower, decelerate); reverse on close |
| **Drawer** | slide from edge (slower, decelerate) |
| **Toast** | slide+fade in (base, decelerate); auto-dismiss fade-out |
| **Tabs** | indicator slide (base, standard) |
| **Accordion** | height expand + content fade (slow, standard) |
| **Table row** | bg color fade on hover (fast) · selected highlight (instant) |
| **Skeleton** | shimmer loop (1500ms, linear, infinite) |
| **Spinner** | rotation loop (1000ms, linear, infinite) |
| **Popover/Dialog** | scale+fade from origin (slow, decelerate) |

---

## Updated Per-Component Checklist (Motion items added)
- [ ] All state variants exist (incl. Open/Closed where applicable)
- [ ] Interactive Component reactions wired (Hover, Click, Press, Mouse Up, Click-outside)
- [ ] Smart Animate uses duration + easing from `Component/{Name}/motion/*` variables
- [ ] Open/Close use opposite easings (decelerate-in, accelerate-out)
- [ ] Loading/loop animations defined (spinner, skeleton)

---

## Why Variable-Driven Motion Wins
- **Slow everything down** for demos: edit `duration/base` 200→500 once
- **Disable motion** (accessibility): set all durations to 0 in one collection
- **Per-component tweak**: change Modal duration without touching Toast
- **Brand feel**: swap `ease/standard` to `ease/spring` everywhere for playful product

---

Now the system has 4 token dimensions: **Color · Shadow · Motion · Typography** — all following the same 3-tier alias chain. Ready to start Phase 0 + 1.1?
button should be builded before input fields right?

Yes — Button comes before Input, and the plan already reflects that (Button is #5, Input is #9).

## Why Button Before Input

Input fields often **embed buttons** as part of their anatomy:

| Input feature | Embedded element |
|---------------|-----------------|
| Clearable input (× to clear) | Icon button |
| Search input (magnifier action) | Icon button |
| Password input (eye toggle) | Icon button |
| Number stepper input (+/−) | 2 icon buttons |
| Input with action ("Apply", "Send") | Text button |

If you build Input first without Button ready, you'd either:
- Hard-code icon SVGs inside Input (breaks atomic rule), or
- Build it without those features and retrofit later (rework)

## Atom Build Order Recap (with dependencies)

```
1. Icon          → (foundation)
2. Text Label    → (foundation)
3. Divider       → (primitive)
4. Spinner       → introduces brand tokens
5. Button        → uses Icon + Text + Spinner          ◄── built first
6. Checkbox      → uses Icon (check/dash)
7. Radio
8. Toggle/Switch
9. Input Field   → uses Icon + Text + Button           ◄── then this
10. Avatar
11. Badge
12. Tag/Chip     → uses Icon + Text (close = icon button optional)
13. Tooltip
```

So order is correct. Ready to begin **Phase 0 + Phase 1.1**?
data.reasoningText: Looking at the build order, Button at #5 should come before Input at #9, especially since input fields often need button components for things like search icons or password toggles. Button is also a foundational component that uses Icon, Text, and Spinner, so it makes sense to establish it first.

data.content: drodpwn and all comes under input? drodpwon on click menu?

Good question — let me clarify the relationship. **Dropdown is NOT an Input** in the atomic sense, but it composes Input + Menu together. Here's the breakdown:

---

## The Three Distinct Components

| # | Component | What it is | Where it lives |
|---|-----------|-----------|----------------|
| **A** | **Input Field** (atom) | The text-entry box itself | Phase 3 — Atom #9 |
| **B** | **Menu** (molecule) | The floating list of options | Phase 4 — Molecule #15 (Dropdown Menu) |
| **C** | **Select / Dropdown** (molecule) | Input that opens a Menu on click | Phase 4 — Molecule #16 (Select single) |

---

## Anatomy of a Select (Dropdown)

```
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ Select country        ▼      │  ← This row is a styled Input Field
└──────────────────────────────┘
              ↓ on click, opens ↓
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│  India                       │
│  United States              │  ← This panel is a Menu (Dropdown Menu)
│  United Kingdom             │     made of Menu Items
│  Germany                    │
└──────────────────────────────┘
```

**Composition**:
```
Select = Input Field (trigger) + Menu (dropdown panel)
        Menu = stack of Menu Items + Divider
        Menu Item = Icon + Text + (optional Checkbox + Badge)
```

---

## Why This Separation Matters

**Menu is its OWN component** because it's used in many places, not just Select:

| Where Menu is used | What triggers it |
|--------------------|-----------------|
| Select (single)    | Input Field click |
| Select (multi)     | Input Field click |
| Search bar autocomplete | Input typing |
| Date Picker | Input + calendar icon |
| Top bar user menu | Avatar click |
| Top bar notifications | Icon button click |
| Table row actions ("⋮") | Icon button click |
| Right-click context menu | Right-click anywhere |
| Combobox / Tag picker | Input click |

If Menu were *inside* Select, you'd duplicate it for every other use case. So Menu lives independently and gets *attached* to whatever trigger needs it.

---

## Updated Build Order (already in plan)

```
Phase 3 — Atoms:
  9.  Input Field        ← the text-entry box only

Phase 4 — Molecules:
  14. Menu Item          ← single row (Icon + Text + Badge + Checkbox)
  15. Dropdown Menu      ← stack of Menu Items + Divider
  16. Select (single)    ← Input Field + Dropdown Menu, on-click open
  17. Select (multi)     ← Input Field + Dropdown Menu (with Checkboxes) + Tags
  18. Search Bar         ← Input Field + Icon + Button (+ optional Menu for autocomplete)
  19. Date Picker        ← Input Field + Button + Icon + calendar grid (uses popover pattern)
```

---

## Motion Tie-in

The Select's open/close interaction comes from the **Menu's motion tokens**:
- `Menu/motion/open/duration` → `duration/slow` (300ms)
- `Menu/motion/open/easing` → `ease/decelerate`
- `Menu/motion/close/easing` → `ease/accelerate`

Click Input Field → Smart Animate transitions Select variant `Closed → Open` → Menu fades+slides down. Click outside → reverse.

---

## So to Answer Directly

- **Dropdown is not under Input** — it's a *molecule that composes* Input + Menu
- **Menu opens on click** of the Input Field (or Button, Avatar, etc.)
- **Menu is reusable** — same component drives Select, autocomplete, user menu, table actions, context menus

Ready to start Phase 0?

toolRequests.arguments:
{"command": "str_replace", "new_str": "## Just-In-Time Token Creation
Don't pre-create all tokens. Add to _Primitives / _Appearance / _Theme only when a component needs them. Keeps library clean and reduces upfront work.

## Layer & Frame Naming Convention (mandatory)
Clean naming = scannable layers panel + reliable text override preservation when swapping variants + clear dev handoff.

### Master Component Naming (slash hierarchy)
Format: `Category/ComponentName/Variant/SubVariant/State`
- `Button/Primary/Medium/Default`
- `Button/Primary/Medium/Hover`
- `Button/Outline/Small/Disabled`
- `Input/Text/Default`
- `Input/Text/Error`
- `Menu/Item/With-Icon/Hover`
- `Modal/Confirm/Danger`

Internal-only atoms (not for designers): prefix with `_` → `_Button/_Inner-Container` (excluded from publish).

### Pages (use emoji + sentence case for browsability)
- `🏠 Cover`
- `📐 Foundations / Tokens`
- `🔤 Typography`
- `🎨 Colors`
- `📏 Spacing & Layout`
- `✨ Effects`
- `🎯 Icons`
- `🧱 Atoms`
- `🧬 Molecules`
- `🏛️ Organisms`
- `📄 Patterns`
- `📝 Documentation`

### Frames (sections inside pages)
PascalCase or Title Case with optional purpose suffix:
- `Button — All Variants`
- `Input / Text — States`
- `Color Tokens — Light Mode`
- `Spec / Anatomy`
- `Spec / Do & Don't`

### Layer Naming Inside Components (semantic, NOT visual)
Use ROLE not appearance. Critical: text override preservation across variant swaps depends on identical layer names.

| Bad | Good |
|-----|------|
| `Rectangle 12` | `Container` or `Background` |
| `Group 4` | `Content` or `Inner` |
| `Frame 234` | `Icon-Leading` |
| `Text` | `Label` |
| `Image 5` | `Avatar-Image` |
| `Vector` | inside Icon comp: keep as-is, or `Icon-Glyph` |

### Standard Layer Roles (use these names consistently)
| Role | Use for |
|------|---------|
| `Container` | outermost frame of the component |
| `Background` | fill layer behind content |
| `Content` | inner auto-layout holding children |
| `Icon-Leading` | icon on the left |
| `Icon-Trailing` | icon on the right |
| `Icon-Action` | clickable icon (close, expand) |
| `Label` | primary text |
| `Description` / `Helper` | secondary/helper text |
| `Placeholder` | placeholder text in inputs |
| `Value` | filled-in text in inputs/selects |
| `Prefix` / `Suffix` | text before/after value (e.g., `$`, `.com`) |
| `Indicator` | check mark, dot, badge dot |
| `Divider` | separator line |
| `Header` / `Body` / `Footer` | for cards, modals, drawers |
| `Title` / `Subtitle` | inside Header |
| `Actions` | container for buttons (modal footer, card footer) |
| `Trigger` | the clickable element that opens a menu/popover |
| `Panel` / `Surface` | floating container (menu panel, tooltip surface) |
| `Backdrop` / `Overlay` | dimming layer behind modal/drawer |
| `Track` / `Thumb` | for sliders, toggles, scrollbars |
| `Fill` | progress bar fill |

### Component Property Naming
- Boolean: `Has Icon Leading`, `Has Icon Trailing`, `Disabled`, `Loading`, `Error`
- Variant: `Variant` (Primary/Secondary/...), `Size` (Small/Medium/Large), `State` (Default/Hover/...)
- Instance Swap: `Icon Leading`, `Icon Trailing`, `Avatar`
- Text: `Label`, `Description`, `Placeholder`, `Value`, `Helper Text`

Use Title Case for property names (Figma displays them this way).

### Variable/Token Naming (already in use above)
- Lowercase, slash-separated: `surface/base`, `text/primary`, `Button/Primary/bg/default`
- Component variables: PascalCase component, lowercase rest: `Card/shadow/blur`, `Modal/backdrop/color`

### Text Style Naming (slash hierarchy for picker grouping)
- `Display/2XL`, `Display/XL`, `Display/L`
- `Heading/H1`, `Heading/H2`, ...
- `Body/Large`, `Body/Default`, `Body/Small`
- `Label/Default`, `Label/Small`
- `Code/Default`, `Code/Small`
- `Brand/Display`, `Brand/Heading` (Puvi-based)

### Effect Style Naming
- `Elevation/1`, `Elevation/2`, ... `Elevation/5`
- `Focus/Ring`
- `Glow/Brand`

### Color Style / Variable Group Naming
- `Surface/Base`, `Surface/Card`, `Surface/Raised`
- `Text/Primary`, `Text/Secondary`
- `Border/Subtle`, `Border/Default`, `Border/Strong`
- `Brand/Primary`, `Brand/On-Primary`
- `Status/Success/Default`, `Status/Error/Subtle`

### Critical Rules
1. **Same layer name across all variants** → text overrides survive variant switches
2. **No "Frame 123" / "Group 45" / "Rectangle"** in any published component
3. **Atoms inside molecules retain their original names** (don't rename Button instances)
4. **Internal scaffolding prefixed `_`** to hide from publish + signal "do not touch"
5. **Layer order matches reading order**: top-to-bottom in panel = left-to-right / top-to-bottom visually", "old_str": "## Just-In-Time Token Creation
Don't pre-create all tokens. Add to _Primitives / _Appearance / _Theme only when a component needs them. Keeps library clean and reduces upfront work.", "path": "/memories/design-system-tokens.md"}

data.content:
Saved. Here's the **OM Design System Naming Convention**:

---

## 1. Master Component Naming
Slash hierarchy → groups in instance picker:
```
Button/Primary/Medium/Default
Button/Primary/Medium/Hover
Button/Outline/Small/Disabled
Input/Text/Error
Menu/Item/With-Icon/Hover
Modal/Confirm/Danger
```
Internal-only atoms: prefix `_` → `_Button/_Inner-Container` (excluded from publish)

## 2. Pages
```
🏠 Cover
📐 Foundations / Tokens
🔤 Typography
🎨 Colors
📏 Spacing & Layout
✨ Effects
🎯 Icons
🧱 Atoms
🧬 Molecules
🏛️ Organisms
📄 Patterns
📝 Documentation
```

## 3. Frames (sections inside pages)
- `Button — All Variants`
- `Input / Text — States`
- `Color Tokens — Light Mode`
- `Spec / Anatomy`
- `Spec / Do & Don't`

## 4. Layer Names Inside Components
**Use ROLE, not visual.** Same name across all variants (preserves text overrides on swap).

| ❌ Bad | ✅ Good |
|--------|---------|
| `Rectangle 12` | `Container` / `Background` |
| `Group 4` | `Content` |
| `Frame 234` | `Icon-Leading` |
| `Text` | `Label` |
| `Image 5` | `Avatar-Image` |

## 5. Standard Layer Roles (use these names everywhere)
| Role | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `Container` | outermost frame |
| `Background` | fill behind content |
| `Content` | inner auto-layout |
| `Icon-Leading` / `Icon-Trailing` / `Icon-Action` | icons by position/role |
| `Label` | primary text |
| `Description` / `Helper` | secondary text |
| `Placeholder` / `Value` | input states |
| `Prefix` / `Suffix` | text adornments |
| `Indicator` | check, dot, badge |
| `Divider` | separator |
| `Header` / `Body` / `Footer` | for cards, modals |
| `Title` / `Subtitle` | inside Header |
| `Actions` | button container |
| `Trigger` | element that opens menu/popover |
| `Panel` / `Surface` | floating container |
| `Backdrop` / `Overlay` | dimming layer |
| `Track` / `Thumb` / `Fill` | sliders, toggles, progress |

## 6. Component Properties (Title Case)
- **Boolean**: `Has Icon Leading`, `Has Icon Trailing`, `Disabled`, `Loading`, `Error`
- **Variant**: `Variant`, `Size`, `State`
- **Instance Swap**: `Icon Leading`, `Icon Trailing`, `Avatar`
- **Text**: `Label`, `Description`, `Placeholder`, `Value`, `Helper Text`

## 7. Variables / Tokens
- Lowercase slash: `surface/base`, `text/primary`
- Component vars: `Button/Primary/bg/default`, `Card/shadow/blur`

## 8. Text Styles
`Display/2XL`, `Heading/H1`, `Body/Default`, `Label/Small`, `Code/Default`, `Brand/Display` (Puvi)

## 9. Effect Styles
`Elevation/1` … `Elevation/5`, `Focus/Ring`, `Glow/Brand`

## 10. Critical Rules
1. **Same layer name across all variants** → text overrides survive swap
2. **No `Frame 123` / `Group 45` / `Rectangle`** in any published component
3. **Atoms inside molecules keep original names** (don't rename Button instances)
4. **Internal scaffolding prefixed `_`** → hides from publish, signals "do not edit"
5. **Layer order matches reading order** → top-to-bottom in panel = top-to-bottom / left-to-right visually

---

Saved to memory — I'll enforce these names when guiding every component build. Ready to start Phase 0?

### Phase 5a — Table Sub-Spec
Tables get their own sub-phase due to complexity. Two layout modes + many column types.

**Layout variants:**
- `Table/Full-Page` — fills viewport, page-level scroll, sticky header on page scroll

**Density variants:** Compact / Default / Comfortable

**Row states:** Default · Hover · Selected · Expanded · Disabled · Loading-skeleton · Error

**Column components (each is its own component, used inside Table cells):**
- `Table/Column/Text` — plain text cell
- `Table/Column/Number` — right-aligned, monospace optional
- `Table/Column/Currency` — formatted with prefix/suffix

### Phase 6 — Patterns
Login · Sign-up · Dashboard · Settings · Data Table page · Onboarding flow · Empty/Error states (404, 500, no-data) · Analytics dashboard with charts

## Component-Specific Variant Notes (locked decisions)
- **Tabs**: Line variant = primary (default underline indicator); Boxy = secondary (contained pill-row); Pill = tertiary (rounded chip)

- **Pickers**: Date / Range / Time / DateTime are 4 separate components (sharing calendar/time atoms)

Avatar/Image is seperate coponent why includeed in file uploads, just u think and work dont copy paste what i gave

You're right — I conflated two different things. Let me fix it.

---

## The Mistake
I lumped "avatar/image upload" as a File Upload variant. But:
- **Avatar** is a **display component** (shows a user's image/initials/icon)
- **File Upload** is an **input mechanism** (lets users pick/drop files)

They're different concerns and shouldn't be variants of each other.

---

## The Fix

**Avatar** stays as Atom #10 — purely for display (image/initials/icon, with status dot, sizes, group/stacked).

**File Upload** becomes only what it actually is — a file input mechanism with two real variants:

| File Upload variant | Use case |
|---------------------|----------|
| **Single File** | Pick/drop one file (e.g., resume, document, single image) |
| **Multiple Files** | Pick/drop many files with a file list (each file = Icon + Filename + Progress + Remove icon-button) |

That's it. No "Avatar Upload" sub-variant.

---

## Where Image Upload Actually Belongs

When a user needs to **upload + change their profile picture**, that's a **pattern**, not a component. It's composed of:
- **Avatar** (display — shows current image)
- **File Upload (Single)** (input — pick new image)
- **Button** (action — "Change", "Remove")
- Optionally: a Modal with a crop tool

This belongs in **Phase 6 — Patterns** as `Pattern / Profile Image Edit` — not as a component.

---

## Updated Plan Entries

```
Atom #10:        Avatar — display only (image/initials/icon, sizes, status dot, group)
Organism #41:    File Upload
                  ├─ Single File
                  └─ Multiple Files (with file list)
Pattern:         Profile Image Edit (Avatar + File Upload + Button + optional crop Modal)
```

data.content:
Fixed and saved. Good catch — keeping concerns separate means each component stays single-purpose and reusable.

This is a useful general principle to lock in:

> **Display ≠ Input.** A component that *shows* something (Avatar, Badge, Tag) is different from a component that *captures* something (File Upload, Input, Select). Don't merge them as variants of each other — compose them in patterns when needed.

Great call — I'm in **Plan mode** so I can't write the plugin code yet, but here's the full bootstrap plugin plan. Once you approve, you can hit handoff to implementation and I'll build it. 

---

## Plan: OM Design System Bootstrap Plugin

A one-shot Figma plugin that, when run, sets up the **entire Phase 0 + Phase 1.1 foundation** in seconds — no manual clicking.

### What It Will Create

**1. Pages (12)** — with emoji names
- 🏠 Cover · 📐 Foundations / Tokens · 🔤 Typography · 🎨 Colors · 📏 Spacing & Layout · ✨ Effects · 🎯 Icons · 🧱 Atoms · 🧬 Molecules · 🏛️ Organisms · 📄 Patterns · 📝 Documentation

**2. Cover Page Content** — title frame with "OM Design System / v0.1.0 — Foundations / April 2026"

**3. 7 Variable Collections** with correct modes:
- `_Primitives` (no modes)
- `_Appearance` (Light, Dark)
- `_Theme` (Orange, Blue) — *empty for now per JIT rule*
- `Typography` (no modes)
- `Spacing` (no modes)
- `Radius` (no modes)
- `Shadows` (Light, Dark) — *empty for now*

**4. Seed Tokens** (Phase 1.1 minimum):

`_Primitives`:

{"content": "# OM DS Bootstrap — Figma Plugin

One-shot Figma plugin that scaffolds **Phase 0 + Phase 1.1** of the OM Design System: pages, variable collections, seed tokens, text styles, and a Light/Dark test frame.

## What It Creates

### 12 Pages
🏠 Cover · 📐 Foundations / Tokens · 🔤 Typography · 🎨 Colors · 📏 Spacing & Layout · ✨ Effects · 🎯 Icons · 🧱 Atoms · 🧬 Molecules · 🏛️ Organisms · 📄 Patterns · 📝 Documentation

### 7 Variable Collections
| Collection | Modes | Tokens seeded |
|-----------|-------|---------------|
| `_Primitives` | Default | `neutral/0..1000` (13 shades) + `transparent` |
| `_Appearance` | Light, Dark | `surface/base`, `surface/card`, `text/primary`, `text/secondary`, `border/default` (aliases to neutrals) |
| `_Theme` | Orange, Blue | *(empty shell — JIT, added with Spinner)* |
| `Typography` | Default | `font/sans`=Inter, `font/serif`=Zoho Puvi, `font/mono`=Roboto Mono |
| `Spacing` | Default | `space/0..160` (22 values) |
| `Radius` | Default | `radius/none..full` (9 values) |
| `Shadows` | Light, Dark | *(empty shell — JIT, added with Card)* |

### 6 Text Styles
`Heading/H1`, `Heading/H2`, `Body/Default`, `Body/Small`, `Label/Default`, `Code/Default`

### Cover Frame
Title card on 🏠 Cover page (1440×900).

### Theme Test Frame
Side-by-side Light + Dark frames on 📐 Foundations page — uses bound variables to verify mode switching works.

---

## How to Use

1. **Open Figma** and create a new design file named `OM Design System`
2. Top menu → **Plugins → Development → Import plugin from manifest…**
3. Select `manifest.json` from this folder
4. Run: **Plugins → Development → OM DS Bootstrap → Bootstrap Phase 1.1 (Foundations)**
5. Wait ~5 seconds — done ✅

The plugin is **idempotent**: re-running skips entities that already exist.

### Reset Command
If you want to start fresh, run **Plugins → Development → OM DS Bootstrap → Reset (Delete OM Collections)**. This removes the 7 collections only; pages, text styles, and frames are kept.

---

## Notes

- **Fonts**: If Zoho Puvi or specific font weights aren't installed locally, the plugin falls back to Inter Regular for text styles. Install Zoho Puvi later → swap the `font/serif` variable value once → cascades everywhere.
- **Brand tokens**: Intentionally omitted from this bootstrap (JIT principle). Added in the next plugin run when we build the Spinner atom.
- **Shadow tokens**: Same — added when we build the Card.

## Future Commands (planned)
- `Add Brand Tokens` — Orange + Blue scales + `brand/*` semantics
- `Add Status Tokens` — Red/Green/Amber/Sky scales + `status/*`
- `Add Shadow Tokens` — elevation primitives + `_Appearance.elevation/*`
- `Add Motion Tokens` — durations + easings
- `Import ZCat-Icons` — bulk icon component import

---

## Troubleshooting

**"Cannot find module" / plugin won't load**: ensure `manifest.json` and `code.js` are in the same folder.

**Fonts missing error**: install Inter and Roboto Mono on your machine, then re-run.

**Variables already exist**: safe — the plugin skips existing variables. To start over, run the **Reset** command.

{"command": "create", "file_text": "# Design System — Token Architecture (Figma Variables)

## Reference Chain (only `_Primitives` holds hex)
```
_Primitives (raw hex) → _Appearance / _Theme (semantic alias) → Component/{Name} (per-component alias) → Component layers
```
Component layers MUST alias `Component/{Name}` only — never `_Primitives` or semantic directly.

## Split-Mode Pattern (Figma Pro = 4 modes/collection)
- `_Primitives` (no modes): raw color scales
- `_Appearance` (Light, Dark): `surface/*`, `text/*`, `border/*`, `status/*`, hover/active overlays
- `_Theme` (Orange, Blue, ...): `brand/*`, `state/focus-ring`
- Frame independently sets _Appearance + _Theme → N×M effective combinations
- Avoids 4-mode-combined-collection (which maxes Pro plan, doubles duplication)

## Brand × Appearance Auto-Switch Pattern
Put brand SCALES in _Theme; let _Appearance pick which scale:
```
_Theme (Orange):  brand/scale-strong → orange/600    brand/scale-soft → orange/400
_Theme (Blue):    brand/scale-strong → blue/600      brand/scale-soft → blue/400
_Appearance (Light):  brand/primary → brand/scale-strong
_Appearance (Dark):   brand/primary → brand/scale-soft
Component/Button: Primary/bg/default → brand/primary  (resolves correctly per Appearance×Theme)
```

## Per-Component Variable Collections (ZCatalyst-style)
Each component owns a `Component/{Name}` collection. Naming: `{element}/{property}/{state}`.
Example Component/Button: `Primary/bg/default`, `Primary/bg/hover`, `Primary/text/default`, `Outline/border/default`.

## Atomic Composition Rule (non-negotiable)
- Higher components use INSTANCES of lower components (Modal's button = Button instance, etc.)
- Every icon = instance of single Icon master (name/size/color properties)
- Edit source atom once → all parents update automatically
- Internal-only atoms prefixed `_` to hide from publish

## Typography Variables (mode-based font swap)
- Single `Typography` collection with 2 MODES: Inter (default) | Puvi
- Variables: `font/sans` (Inter or Zoho Puvi by mode), `font/mono` (always Roboto Mono)
- All text styles bind Font Family field to `font/sans` (not typed names)
- Line-height baked INTO the text style; override per-component only when layout demands
- Switch the Typography mode (frame-level) → entire system swaps Inter ↔ Puvi instantly
- No separate font/serif — Puvi is the alt UI font accessed via mode flip
- Rule: every text layer must use a defined text style; manual font overrides break the swap

## Shadows / Effects (same per-component pattern)
Both shadow COLOR and shadow VALUES (offset/blur/spread) are variables.
```
_Primitives:
  shadow/color/black-soft = rgba(0,0,0,0.08)
  shadow/color/black-med  = rgba(0,0,0,0.16)
  shadow/color/black-hard = rgba(0,0,0,0.40)
  shadow/blur/sm=4 md=12 lg=24
  shadow/y/sm=1 md=4 lg=12
  shadow/spread/0=0 -2=-2

_Appearance (Light): elevation/card/color → shadow/color/black-soft
_Appearance (Dark):  elevation/card/color → shadow/color/black-hard

Component/Card:
  Card/shadow/color  → elevation/card/color
  Card/shadow/y      → shadow/y/md
  Card/shadow/blur   → shadow/blur/md
  Card/shadow/spread → shadow/spread/-2
```
Each elevated component (Modal, Dropdown, Tooltip, Popover, Toast) owns its shadow tokens in its Component/{Name} collection.

## Motion / Interaction Variables (same 3-tier pattern)
```
_Primitives (Number):
  duration/instant=0  fast=100  base=200  slow=300  slower=400  slowest=600  (ms)
_Primitives (String):
  ease/linear, ease/standard, ease/decelerate (entering), ease/accelerate (exiting), ease/spring

Component/Button:
  Button/motion/hover/duration → duration/fast
  Button/motion/hover/easing   → ease/standard
  Button/motion/press/scale    → 0.97
```
Implementation: Variants for all states + Smart Animate + Interactive Component reactions (Hover/Click/Press/Mouse Up). Open=decelerate, Close=accelerate.

## Standard Motion Catalog
Button: hover color fade (fast), press scale 0.97 (instant), focus ring fade (fast)
Input/Select: focus border + glow (base)
Checkbox/Radio: check-in scale+fade (base); Toggle: thumb slide (base, spring)
Dropdown/Menu: open slide+fade (slow, decelerate); close fade (fast, accelerate)
Tooltip: appear with delay (fade+slide, fast)
Modal: backdrop fade + content scale 0.95→1 (slower, decelerate)
Drawer: slide from edge (slower, decelerate)
Toast: slide+fade in (base, decelerate); auto-dismiss
Tabs: indicator slide (base, standard)
Accordion: height expand + fade (slow, standard)
Table row: bg fade on hover (fast); selected highlight (instant)
Skeleton: shimmer loop 1500ms linear infinite
Spinner: rotation loop 1000ms linear infinite

## Just-In-Time Token Creation
Don't pre-create all tokens. Add to _Primitives / _Appearance / _Theme only when a component needs them.

## Layer & Frame Naming Convention

### Master Components (slash hierarchy)
`Category/ComponentName/Variant/SubVariant/State` — e.g., `Button/Primary/Medium/Default`, `Input/Text/Error`, `Menu/Item/With-Icon/Hover`
Internal-only: prefix `_` → `_Button/_Inner-Container` (excluded from publish)

### Pages (emoji + sentence case)
🏠 Cover · 📐 Foundations / Tokens · 🔤 Typography · 🎨 Colors · 📏 Spacing & Layout · ✨ Effects · 🎯 Icons · 🧱 Atoms · 🧬 Molecules · 🏛️ Organisms · 📄 Patterns · 📝 Documentation

### Frames
PascalCase or Title Case: `Button — All Variants`, `Spec / Anatomy`, `Spec / Do & Don't`

### Layer Names (ROLE not visual; same name across variants → text overrides survive swap)
Bad → Good:
- Rectangle 12 → Container / Background
- Group 4 → Content
- Frame 234 → Icon-Leading
- Text → Label
- Image 5 → Avatar-Image

### Standard Layer Roles
Container, Background, Content, Icon-Leading, Icon-Trailing, Icon-Action, Label, Description/Helper, Placeholder, Value, Prefix/Suffix, Indicator, Divider, Header/Body/Footer, Title/Subtitle, Actions, Trigger, Panel/Surface, Backdrop/Overlay, Track/Thumb, Fill

### Component Properties (Title Case)
- Boolean: `Has Icon Leading`, `Disabled`, `Loading`, `Error`
- Variant: `Variant`, `Size`, `State`
- Instance Swap: `Icon Leading`, `Icon Trailing`, `Avatar`
- Text: `Label`, `Description`, `Placeholder`, `Value`, `Helper Text`

### Variable Names
Lowercase slash: `surface/base`, `text/primary`, `Button/Primary/bg/default`, `Card/shadow/blur`

### Text Style Names (slash hierarchy for picker grouping)
Display/2XL · Heading/H1..H5 · Body/Large|Default|Small · Label/Default|Small · Code/Default|Small · Brand/Display|Heading (Puvi)

### Effect Style Names
Elevation/1..5 · Focus/Ring · Glow/Brand

### Critical Rules
1. Same layer name across all variants → text overrides survive
2. No "Frame 123" / "Group 45" / "Rectangle" in published components
3. Atoms inside molecules retain original names (don't rename Button instances)
4. Internal scaffolding prefixed `_` → hide from publish + signal "do not edit"
5. Layer order matches reading order

## Figma Plan Mode Limits
| Plan | Modes per collection |
|------|---------------------|
| Starter (Free) | 1 |
| Professional | 4 |
| Organization / Enterprise | unlimited |

for all typo. u have to use component right? sample atttached dont copy in styles?

Good question. Let me clarify the two approaches and recommend the right one.

---

## Two Approaches to Typography in Figma

### Option A — Text Styles (what ZCatalyst uses, what we're doing)
- Each style (H1, Body, Label) is a **Figma Text Style** (registered in Styles panel)
- Designer applies via right-panel Text Styles dropdown
- ✅ Standard practice, used by MUI, Ant, Polaris, ZCatalyst (your screenshot)
- ✅ Bound to font variables → mode swap works
- ✅ Lightweight, native Figma feature

### Option B — Text as Components
- Each style is a Figma Component (`Text/H1`, `Text/Body/Default`)
- Designer drags from Assets panel, edits text via property
- ❌ Heavier (every text instance = component instance in layer tree)
- ❌ Awkward — text editing requires entering instance
- ❌ Not industry standard
- Only used when you need things like: built-in semantic HTML wrapping, complex multi-line behavior, dev-handoff annotations baked in

---

## What ZCatalyst Did (Your Screenshot)
Look at the right panel: **Text styles** section with `H1 · 40/Auto`, `H2 · 32/Auto`, `Body 1 · 14/20` etc. These are **Text Styles** — exactly Option A. Same approach we're already using.

---

## What Belongs as a Component
- ❌ H1, H2, Body — NOT components (use Text Styles)
- ✅ **Text Label** atom — a component that *uses* a text style + adds layout, color, truncation, alignment props (this is what we planned for Atom #2)

Difference: Text Style = the typography spec. Text Label component = a reusable text element with extra logic.

---

## What You'll Build Going Forward
1. Today (already done) — basic text styles via plugin: H1, H2, Body/Default, Body/Small, Label/Default, Code/Default
2. Next plugin run — expand to full 18-style catalog: Display 2XL→L, Heading H1→H5, Body L/D/S, Label D/S, Caption, Code D/S, Brand Display/Heading
3. Atom #2 (`Text Label` component) — wraps these styles + exposes color, alignment, truncation, max-lines, optional icon, as Figma component properties

---

## So You're Right
Don't build H1/H2/Body as styles AND components — that's duplication. Use **Text Styles** (Option A, current approach). Atomic components (Text Label, Button label, etc.) reference those styles internally.

## Recommended Min Font Size: **12px**

10px is too small for UI body text (fails WCAG readability for many users). 12px is the practical minimum used by MUI, Ant, IBM Carbon. Use it for tiny labels, helper text, captions in tables.

Good thinking. Let me lay out the recommendations:

---

## Font Sizes for Small Components

| Component | Recommended Size | Style to Use |
|-----------|-----------------|--------------|
| **Badge** (count, dot label) | 12px | `Label/Default` (12/16, Medium) |
| **Tag/Chip** | 12px | `Label/Default` |
| **Tooltip** | 12px | `Label/Default` |
| **Helper text** (under inputs) | 12px | `Label/Default` |
| **Table cell — compact density** | 12px | `Label/Default` |
| **Table cell — default density** | 14px | `Body/Default` |
| **Form label** (above input) | 12px | `Label/Default` |
| **Caption / metadata** | 12px | `Label/Default` |

**12px is the floor.** Don't go lower. Anything that "needs" 11 or 10 should re-evaluate hierarchy instead.

---

## Buttons → Use Medium Weight (Yes, Smart Idea)

You're right — buttons need slightly heavier text than body for visual weight and clickability cues. This is what MUI, Ant, Polaris all do.

### Recommended Button Text Styles (new dedicated styles)

| Button Size | Style Name | Font Size / LH | Weight |
|-------------|-----------|----------------|--------|
| Small | `Button/Small` | 12 / 16 | **Medium (500)** |
| Medium *(default)* | `Button/Default` | 14 / 20 | **Medium (500)** |
| Large | `Button/Large` | 16 / 24 | **Medium (500)** |

Why dedicated styles (not reuse Body/Default):
- Body is **Regular (400)**; buttons need **Medium (500)** for weight contrast
- Different letter-spacing optimal for buttons (slightly tighter, ~0%)
- If you ever want bolder buttons system-wide, edit one style → all buttons update

### Same Pattern for Other Interactive Elements

| Element | Style |
|---------|-------|
| Tab label | `Label/Default` (Medium 12) for compact, `Body/Default` Medium variant for default |
| Menu Item | `Body/Default` (Regular 14) — selected state can swap to Medium |
| Nav link | `Body/Default` (Regular 14) — active state Medium |

---

## Updated Text Style Catalog (Final)

**18 total styles** — adding 3 button styles, dropping 3 odd-numbered ones:

| # | Style | Size / LH | Weight | Use |
|---|-------|-----------|--------|-----|
| 1 | Display/2XL | 60 / 72 | Bold | Hero |
| 2 | Display/XL | 48 / 56 | Bold | Hero |

| 9 | Body/Large | 16 / 24 | Regular | Article |
| 10 | Body/Default | 14 / 20 | Regular | UI default |
| 11 | Label/Default | 12 / 16 | Medium | Captions, helper, badges, tags, tooltips |
| 12 | **Button/Small** | 12 / 16 | **Medium** | Small button text |
| 13 | **Button/Default** | 14 / 20 | **Medium** | Default button text |
| 14 | **Button/Large** | 16 / 24 | **Medium** | Large button text |
| 15 | Code/Default | 14 / 20 | Regular (mono) | Code blocks |
| 16 | Code/Small | 12 / 16 | Regular (mono) | Inline code |
| 17 | Brand/Display | 48 / 56 | Bold (Puvi) | Brand hero |
| 18 | Brand/Heading | 32 / 40 | Semi Bold (Puvi) | Brand section |

### Spacing — Cleaned (no odd values)
`0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32, 40, 48, 56, 64, 80, 96, 128, 160`

### Radius — Already clean

2. Add: Button/Small (12 Medium), Button/Default (14 Medium), Button/Large (16 Medium)
3. Fix: Code/Default 13→14

Correct — **10px will not be used anywhere** in the system.

## Why
- 10px is too small for accessible UI (WCAG readability concerns)
- MUI, Ant, Polaris, Carbon all set 12px as their floor
- Anything that "needs" 10px usually means the wrong hierarchy — use icon, color, or position to differentiate instead of shrinking text

## Where You Might Be Tempted to Use 10
| Tempting use | Use instead |
|--------------|-------------|
| Tiny badge count "99+" | Label/Default 12 (just keep the badge tight) |
| Table dense compact | Label/Default 12 |
| Photo metadata / EXIF | Label/Default 12 |
| Footer copyright | Label/Default 12 |
| Chart axis labels | Label/Default 12 |
| Chip sub-text | Don't add sub-text to chips — keep them single-line |

**Floor = 12px. Period.**

## Confirmed Catalog
- **Caption (10) — REMOVED** ✅
- Min font size in entire OM Design System = **12px** (Label/Default)

Good catch. Two things going on — let me explain the architecture, then fix it.

## What you're seeing & why

The Color picker shows **`_Appearance`** because in Figma's **local file picker**, ALL local variable collections show up — the `_` prefix only hides them when *another file consumes this as a library*. So inside this file, you'll always see `_Appearance` until we add scope restrictions.

## The intended architecture (3-tier)

```
_Primitives (raw hex)       ← Internal — never apply directly
    ↓
_Appearance / _Theme        ← Semantic — apply to GENERIC surfaces/text only
    ↓
Component/Button/bg etc.    ← Per-component — apply INSIDE that component only
```

**You're correct** — when designing a Button, only `Component/Button/*` should appear. When designing a generic Card or app shell, `_Appearance` (surface/card, text/primary) is allowed.

## Fix: Variable Scopes

Figma supports **scopes** that restrict where a variable can be applied. I'll update the bootstrap to set:

| Variable | Scope (where it can be picked) |
|----------|--------------------------------|
| All `_Primitives` (neutrals + brand scales) | **Hidden from picker entirely** — only usable as alias source |
| `surface/*` | Only on Frame/Shape fills |
| `text/*` | Only on Text fills |
| `border/*` | Only on Strokes |
| `icon/*` | Only on Frame/Shape fills (vectors) |

This means when you click a fill on a text node, you'll only see `text/*` options — not the whole list.

## Component-Specific Variables

Per-component vars (`Button/bg`, `Card/border`, etc.) get created **JIT** when we build each component. They live in their own `Component/{Name}` collection and alias to `_Appearance`/`_Theme`. Designers using a Button will see ONLY `Button/*` choices for that component's slots.

---

Want me to add the scope restrictions now? It'll:
1. Hide all `_Primitives` from the picker (still aliased internally)
2. Restrict `surface`/`text`/`border`/`icon` to their proper paint contexts

Reply "yes" and I'll add it to the bootstrap.

Actually, I'm realizing the underscore naming convention is just our internal signal—Figma doesn't automatically hide these from the local picker. The `hiddenFromPublishing` flag only affects library consumers in other files, not the current file's picker. So within the same file, all local variables remain visible regardless of naming or that flag.

The real issue is that the architecture intends for designers to use Component/* colors as the public interface, with _Appearance and _Primitives as internal implementation details. But since they're all visible in the picker, the protection relies on naming convention and discipline rather than technical enforcement. ## What's Next: Phase 2.5 — Icon Master Component

Now I'll build a single **`Icon`** master component that lets designers:

- Drop one `Icon` instance anywhere
- Change `name` property → swaps to any of the 383 icons via dropdown
- Change `size` variant → 12 / 16 / 20 / 24 / 32 px
- Change `color` → bind to `icon/default`, `icon/strong`, `icon/brand`, `icon/inverse`, etc.

**Caveat**: Figma's instance-swap dropdown with 383 options will work but be unwieldy. The **standard pattern** used by Material/Ant/Polaris is:
- Designers **drag the specific component** from the asset panel (e.g., `Icon/heart`) directly
- The `Icon/*` naming convention groups them automatically in the assets panel

This is actually cleaner than a single master component for very large icon sets.

## Phase 3: Atoms — Starting Now

Per the plan, Phase 3 atoms ship in dependency order:

1. **Divider** — trivial, uses `border/default` only
2. **Spinner** — introduces motion tokens + `brand/primary` *(unlocks _Theme)*
3. **Button** — first major atom, full per-component variables, states, focus ring
4. **Checkbox / Radio / Toggle** — selection controls
5. **Input Field** — introduces `status/error`, `status/success`
6. **Avatar / Badge / Tag** — display atoms
7. **Tooltip** — introduces `surface/inverse`

## Recommendation: Skip ahead to Button

Honestly, Divider and Spinner are 10 minutes each but low-impact. **Button** is the highest-value atom — once Button is solid:
- Brand tokens (`_Theme.brand/*`) are unlocked → Orange/Blue switching works
- Establishes the per-component variable pattern (`Component/Button/*`) that ALL future atoms follow
- You can demo the full design system to anyone

**A. Brand scale primitives** (Orange + Blue, 50-900 ranges)

**C. `Component/Button` collection** with per-variant tokens:
- `Button/Primary/bg/{default,hover,active,disabled}`
- `Button/Primary/text/{default,disabled}`
- `Button/Secondary/bg/...`, `Button/Ghost/bg/...`, `Button/Danger/bg/...`

**D. Component variants** (the actual Button component set):
- `Variant`: Primary / Secondary / Ghost / Danger
- `Size`: Small / Default / Large
- `State`: Default / Hover / Active / Disabled
- `IconLeft`: boolean (instance-swap to Icon/*)
- `IconRight`: boolean

## Key Rules
- NEVER use opacity for disabled state — varies wildly across bg colors. Use solid neutral tokens: `state/disabled-bg`, `state/disabled-text`, `state/disabled-border`.
- Per-component disabled aliases to these neutral state tokens (not brand-muted)."}

data.content:
Done. Summary of fixes:

| Change | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Disabled bg | `brand/primary-muted` (faded orange) | `state/disabled-bg` → `neutral/100` light, `neutral/800` dark |
| Disabled text | `brand/on-primary` (white, invisible on grey) | `state/disabled-text` → `neutral/400` light, `neutral/600` dark |
| Disabled border (Secondary) | brand/primary still showing | `state/disabled-border` → `neutral/200` light, `neutral/800` dark |
| Opacity hack | `0.5` for filled, `0.6` for outline | **Removed** — solid colors only |

Result: disabled buttons will look the same on white, dark, or any colored surface.

## Steps
1. Re-import plugin
2. **Bootstrap Phase 1.1** *(adds 3 new disabled state tokens to _Appearance)*
3. Delete existing Button component set on Atoms page
4. **Build Button** *(now uses neutral disabled tokens)*

You'll see disabled Primary/Danger as solid grey with grey text — proper industry-standard appearance.

data.content: I also need `surface/base` in the required map (it might not be there):

Done. Summary of fixes:

## Disabled Colors (per variant)
| Variant | Disabled bg | Disabled text |
|---------|-------------|---------------|
| **Primary** | `brand/primary-subtle` (faded brand bg) | `brand/primary-muted` (faded brand text) |
| **Secondary** | `surface/card` | `state/disabled-text` (neutral grey) |
| **Ghost** | `surface/card` | `state/disabled-text` |
| **Danger** | `brand/primary-subtle` (will be `danger-subtle` later) | `brand/primary-muted` |

## Showcase
A new **"Button — Showcase"** frame is built next to the component set, with:
- 4 sections (one per variant: Primary, Secondary, Ghost, Danger)
- Each section is a grid: rows = States (Default/Hover/Active/Disabled), columns = Sizes (XS/Small/Medium/Default/Large)
- Title + subtitle explain how to test theming

## Steps
1. Re-import plugin
2. **Bootstrap Phase 1.1** *(safe re-run)*
3. On Atoms page → delete existing `Button` component set AND any old `Button — Showcase` frame
4. **Build Button**

You'll get the component set + a clean docs grid like Blade/MUI. Toggle `_Theme` mode on the Showcase frame → all buttons swap Orange↔Blue.

data.reasoningText:
Looking at the button variant styling, I need to adjust how Secondary and Ghost buttons render by default—they should have transparent backgrounds with just borders, not the white card surface I'm currently using. For the Disabled Primary state, I'm checking the reference and it appears to use a light neutral background with primary-colored text rather than a faded version of the brand color.

I'm also introducing a new Neutral variant for icon-only actions like close buttons and menus, which uses a solid neutral background. So the full structure becomes: Primary with brand colors and a neutral disabled state, Secondary as an outlined variant that goes transparent when disabled, Ghost as fully transparent, this new Neutral for utility actions, and Danger following the same pattern as Primary but in red.

data.content:
Done. Summary of the new behavior:

| Variant | Default bg | Default text | Disabled bg | Disabled text |
|---------|------------|--------------|-------------|---------------|
| **Primary** | brand orange/blue | white | **neutral subtle grey** | low-contrast grey |
| **Secondary** | **transparent** | brand color | transparent | grey text + grey border |
| **Ghost** | **transparent** | brand color | transparent | grey text only |
| **Neutral** ⭐ NEW | solid grey | dark text | grey | low-contrast text |
| **Danger** | brand (red later) | white | neutral subtle grey | low-contrast grey |

Hover states still tint with brand-subtle so users see something happen.

## Steps

1. Re-import plugin
2. Delete existing Button + Showcase from Atoms page

Based on your references, here's what I'd build (3 new components + an extension):

## Plan

### 1. Extend existing `Button` (no new component)
Add `Has Left Icon` + `Has Right Icon` boolean properties + icon swap. This covers:
- **Nav back button** = Ghost variant + LeftIcon=arrow-left + text="Back"
- Any button with leading/trailing icon (chevron-down, plus, etc.)

### 2. New `IconButton` component (icon-only square)
- Variants: **Primary, Secondary (Line), Ghost, Neutral, Danger** (matches Button)
- Sizes: **XS/Small/Medium/Default/Large** = **24/28/32/36/40** square (1:1 aspect, same h as Button so they align in toolbars)
- States: Default/Hover/Active/Disabled
- Icon = Icon component instance, swappable (default: plus)
- Reuses **all existing Button color tokens** (no new tokens needed)
- Total: 5 × 5 × 4 = **100 variants**

### 3. New `SplitButton` component
- Main action segment + chevron-down segment, separated by 1px divider
- Variants: **Primary, Secondary (Line), Neutral** (3 — Ghost/Danger don't make sense as split)
- Sizes: Small/Medium/Default/Large (skip XS — too cramped)
- States: Default/Hover/Active/Disabled
- Both segments hoverable independently in real code; in Figma we'll expose `Active Segment = Main | Chevron` for hover/active variants — OR keep it simple with whole-button states only
- Total: 3 × 4 × 4 = **48 variants** (whole-button states)
- Uses existing tokens; chevron divider = `brand/primary-active` (Primary), `state/disabled-border` (Neutral/Line)

### Question before I code
**Q:** For SplitButton hover/active — do you want the **whole button** to highlight together, or show **per-segment** states (main and chevron highlight independently)?

- **A) Whole-button** — simpler, 48 variants. Click feedback is unified.
- **B) Per-segment** — adds `Segment=Main|Chevron` axis → 96 variants. Realistic since the two segments do different things.

Reply **A** or **B** and I'll build all three (Button extension + IconButton + SplitButton + showcase) in one pass.

Great idea — that's the proper ZCat pattern. Let me restructure into ONE unified Button component:

**New structure:**
- **Type** axis: `Default | Split | Icon`
- **Color, Size, State** axes (as before)
- **Booleans**: `Icon Left` (Default + Split), `Icon Right` (Default only — Split has fixed chevron, Icon has no label)
- Skip nonsensical combos: Split+Ghost, Split+XS, Icon doesn't use booleans

**Variant count**: 120 (Default) + 80 (Split) + 120 (Icon) = **320** — way less than 360+120+80=560.

data.reasoningText:
I'm consolidating the Button component into a single unified structure with a Type axis covering Default, Split, and Icon variants, where icon placement is controlled by boolean toggles rather than an enum—though Split Button restricts to Icon Left only with a fixed chevron, and Icon Button renders as a square with no text content. This is going to create a significant variant matrix across color, size, state, and icon combinations. So I'm settling on 320 total variants across the three types, accounting for the constraints like skipping Ghost color for Split and XS size for Split. The key insight is that boolean properties for icon visibility live outside the variant axes themselves — they're wired to individual nodes within each variant, so I can toggle icon display without creating separate variant combinations. Now I'm ready to implement this structure.

I'll consolidate the button building into a single unified function, deprecating the separate IconButton and SplitButton commands. The Figma plugin API lets me define boolean properties once on the component set, then bind them to specific nodes' visibility across variants using `componentPropertyReferences`. My approach is to generate all the valid variant combinations first, then layer on the boolean properties and wire up the icon visibility toggles. Figma doesn't support per-variant property visibility, so I'll just add the booleans set-wide and let irrelevant ones be no-ops for variants that don't use them. Now I'm rewriting the `buildButton` function to replace the Content axis with the new Type-based structure—this drops the variant count from 360 down to around 320 once I account for the three Type variants. I'll remove the separate menu entries for icon and split buttons to consolidate everything into one unified Button component, though I can keep the underlying functions for backward compatibility.

Apply this **same pattern to all components** (Button done; replicate for Input, Checkbox, Toggle, Card, etc.).

## Layout (Blade/MUI inspired)
- Manual 2D grid: `compSet.layoutMode = 'NONE'`, position variants via `x/y` so Figma renders native purple variant axis labels
- Spacing constants:
  - `COL_W` per size (XS:100, S:120, M:140, D:160, L:180)
  - `ROW_H = 72`, `COLOR_GAP = 56`, `TYPE_GAP = 120`
  - `PAD_LEFT = 180` (state/type labels), `PAD_TOP = 100` (color/size labels)
- Inline labels as page siblings (NOT inside compSet — sets can't hold text):
  - Top: Color group header (`Heading/H4`) + Size sub-headers (`Label/Default`)
  - Left: Type section header (`Heading/H4`) + State row labels (`Label/Default`)
- **No separate showcase frame** — labels live around the compSet
- Wrap compSet + labels + lines in a parent group/frame on Atoms page if needed

## Separators (purple dashed, like Blade)
- Color: `{r:0.55, g:0.45, b:0.95}`, dash `[4,4]`, weight 1
- Vertical between color groups (opacity 0.55)
- Horizontal between type sections (opacity 0.55)
- Faint horizontals between state rows within a type (opacity 0.18)
- Header underline + left header divider (opacity 0.4)

## Disabled state pattern
- Use **default visual tokens** (brand bg + white text), then apply `comp.opacity = 0.45`
- Do NOT use grey-on-grey `state/disabled-bg` + `state/disabled-text` for the buttons
- Tokens for disabled still exist in _Appearance for non-component contexts

## Variant naming
`Type=X, Color=Y, Size=Z, State=W` — order matters for axis label rendering

## Reference implementation
See `buildButton()` in `om-bootstrap-plugin/code.js` (~line 1011 onwards)

Two paths forward — pick one or both:

## Option A — Extract the layout pattern into a reusable helper
Pull the inline-labels + dashed-separators code from `buildButton` into a generic helper like `decorateComponentSet(page, compSet, { types, colors, sizes, states, colorX, typeY, ... })`, so every future component (Checkbox, Radio, Toggle, Input, etc.) gets the same Blade-style grid for free.

## Option B — Move to the next atom
Per `/memories/session/plan.md`, the build order after Button is:
**Checkbox → Radio → Toggle → Input Field → Avatar → Badge → Tag → Tooltip**

- Tokens already in place: `state/disabled-bg`, `state/disabled-border`, `text/primary`, `brand/primary`, `brand/on-primary`, `state/focus-ring`

## Recommended order
1. **Do A first** (small refactor, ~30 min) — extract `decorateComponentSet` helper
2. **Then B** — build Checkbox using the helper, so labels/separators come automatically

data.reasoningText: I'm spotting several styling issues with the button component that need addressing: the padding is too generous when icons are hidden, the divider lines on split buttons aren't matching the border colors properly, and the disabled green state needs work. I should also make sure icon visibility doesn't affect the button's width—it should always fit its content naturally. Let me pull up the Button build code to start fixing these.

Looking at the Split component structure, I can see the comp is resized to 120 width while main gets a fixed 80 width. With primaryAxisSizingMode set to AUTO, the comp should hug its content, but the main element's fixed width might be causing the split width issue. When the icon is disabled, the Icon Left frame stays in the autolayout with visibility off, which could still be affecting how the layout hugs.

Now examining the divider line problem — the code currently uses divider-on-brand for all states, which appears as white on the white background for Secondary buttons and white on grey for Neutral, making it nearly invisible. I need to change this so Secondary uses the brand primary border color and Neutral uses the default border color instead.

For the green disabled button issue, the current color is too pale and the white text doesn't have enough contrast. I should either darken the green shade or switch the text to a darker green for better readability on the disabled state.

On the icon spacing question — when an icon is hidden in Figma's autolayout, it shouldn't reserve space, so that's probably not the issue. Yes — full reset is fine and the cleanest path. Order:

**In Figma:**
1. Run **Reset (Delete OM Collections)** — wipes all variables (`_Primitives`, `_Appearance`, `_Theme`, `Component/Button`, `Component/Checkbox`)
2. Manually delete pages you want fresh: **Atoms** (Button + Checkbox component sets), **Icons**, **Foundations / Tokens**, etc.
3. Run plugin commands in order:
   - **Bootstrap Phase 1.1 (Foundations)** — recreates all variables + pages
   - **Build Icon Components** — 383 icons
   - **Build Button** — 360 variants
   - **Build Checkbox** — 108 variants

That gives you a totally clean state with all the latest token values.

- **Disabled** = not interactive (greyed out, no pointer)

data.reasoningText:
The arrow gap issue is coming from how the polygon rotates — when the triangle rotates 90 degrees for right placement, the visual tip ends up on the left side of its bounding box, creating that detached appearance even though the positioning should be correct.

I'm realizing the real problem: after rotation, the bounding box dimensions don't actually change in the layer properties, but the rendered shape does, which throws off the alignment. Instead of fighting with rotation, I should create the polygon with the correct native orientation from the start — making vertical arrows with a 2:1 width-to-height ratio and horizontal arrows with a 1:2 ratio, so they point in the right direction without needing to rotate. After rotating 90° clockwise, the layer's width and height properties stay the same in Figma, but the visual bounding box rotates around the center—so I need to account for how the positioning shifts after rotation, not just the dimensions. I need to reconsider Figma's rotation direction—positive angles go counter-clockwise, so a -90° rotation will turn an upward-pointing arrow to face right, which is what I need for the left-side placement. Rather than wrestling with the polygon rotation math, I'll just use vectors to define the arrow shapes explicitly for each direction, which avoids all this coordinate confusion. Done. Reload plugin → run **Build Avatar (Single + Group)**.

**What changed:**

1. **Merged into one component** — single `Avatar` set with axes:
   - `Type` = Single | Group
   - For `Single`: Size × Variant(Image/Initials/Icon) × Shape × Status

4. **Showcase** — single grid on top (with all the existing labels), "Grouped (Type=Group)" sub-grid below it with Count columns and Size×Overflow rows. Standalone `AvatarGroup` command/function removed; `Build AvatarGroup` menu item gone.

That's not a bug — it's normal Figma behavior. When you click a component set in the canvas, the **Layers panel expands and shows every variant inside it** by its full variant name (`Type=Default, Color=Danger, Size=Default, State=Hover` etc.). A 460-variant Button set will show 460 rows in that panel. Nothing is duplicated; collapse the `Button` layer to hide them.

**About the floating variant in your earlier screenshot at X=0, Y=0**: that was the variant you'd selected, and `X=0` refers to its position **relative to the parent compSet** (not the page). All variants live at compSet-relative coordinates starting from `(PAD_LEFT, PAD_TOP)`, so `X=0` would be unusual — but in your screenshot the panel showed `X=0, Y=0` of the **selected variant after you'd dragged or scrolled it** out of place, not its baked layout position.

**To verify everything is correct:**
1. Click the **Button** wrapper frame (not a variant inside it).
2. In the Layers panel, **collapse** the `Button` component set node.
3. Visually inspect — the screenshot 1 from earlier already shows the full grid laid out correctly across all 5 type sections.

If you want me to actually fix something specific, tell me **what visual problem** you see (e.g., "variant X overlaps variant Y", "section Z is misaligned"). The console violations and many-named layers are not bugs.

Looking more closely at the variant structure, I see `BUTTON_SIZES` includes five sizes: XS, Small, Medium, Default, and Large — and Medium is indeed a valid size distinct from Default. The real problem appears to be that one variant positioned at coordinates 0,0 with dimensions 100×36 didn't get repositioned by the loop that should be moving all variants into place, possibly because `xFor` is returning NaN for that particular variant.

The layout issue with that one variant at 0,0 might simply be where that selected variant is positioned in the document when you click into the component.

But wait, if the variant is inside the component set, then x=0 should be relative to the set's top-left, which would be a problem since we positioned variants starting at PAD_LEFT=180 and PAD_TOP=100. That variant with Hover state, Danger color, and Default size shouldn't be at relative 0,0 — it should've been repositioned. The bug might be in how the variant metadata is keyed against the actual variants, or there's an indexing mismatch. They're either from the current build but somehow didn't make it into the component set, or they're actually variants within the component set that are just showing up as individual rows in the layers panel — which is totally normal. I think this might just be a UI confusion rather than an actual problem.

but in status we need to show after filled like, after text entered and after drpdown selected chips, single select multi selects, no chips like that and selected many measn +2 options   what to do for that?

Good question — "Filled" isn't one state, it's many. Here's my proposal:

**Sizes** (match Button): XS / Small / Medium / Default / Large

**Type axis** (the major split):
1. **Text** — plain text input
2. **Textarea** — multi-line
3. **Select** — single-select dropdown (chevron on right)
4. **Multi-Select** — chips inside the input + chevron

**State** (interaction): Default / Hover / Active(focused) / Disabled

**Status** (validation): None / Error / Success / Warning

**Content axis** (handles your "filled" question — different per Type):
- For `Text` / `Textarea`: `Empty` (placeholder visible) | `Filled` (real text shown)
- For `Select`: `Empty` (placeholder) | `Filled` (selected label) | `Filled+Icon` (icon prefix on selected item)
- For `Multi-Select`: `Empty` | `1 chip` | `2 chips` | `Overflow (+N)` — the +N pattern from your screenshot

**Optional booleans** (component properties, swap on/off without new variants):

{"command": "create", "path": "/memories/figma-plugin-patterns.md", "file_text": "# Figma Plugin Build Patterns (OM Design System)

## Text styles & fonts
- Always `await figma.loadFontAsync(style.fontName)` before `setTextStyleIdAsync()` AND before setting `.characters`
- NEVER set `text.fontName = {...}` AFTER `setTextStyleIdAsync()` — it breaks the typography link in the design panel
- If you want a specific weight, fix it on the text style itself (don't override on the node)

## Sizing & autolayout gotchas
- Set `layoutSizingHorizontal/Vertical` AFTER `appendChild()` — before that the parent context is unknown
- For frames that should keep a fixed counter-axis after children added, set `counterAxisSizingMode = 'FIXED'` then `layoutSizingVertical = 'FIXED'` and `.resize()` AT THE END (children may force a re-hug otherwise)

## Variable resolution (dynamic-page mode)
- Brand tokens (`brand/*`) live in `_Theme`, not `_Appearance`
- Use a fallback resolver: `anyBy = (n) => appBy(n) || thBy(n)`
- Always `await figma.variables.getLocalVariableCollectionsAsync()` (sync APIs disabled with `documentAccess: dynamic-page`)

## Component-as-instance pattern (atomic composition)
- For atoms reused inside larger components (Label inside TextField, Chip inside Dropdown):
  1. Build the atom first with its own component-set + state variants
  2. Lookup helper: `findXComponent(state)` → searches Atoms page for the COMPONENT_SET, returns the matching variant
  3. Throw `Run "Build X" first` if missing — clear error
  4. Instance via `comp.createInstance()`, name it, then `layoutSizingHorizontal = 'HUG'`
  5. Wire boolean props on the wrapper to toggle the entire instance via `componentPropertyReferences = { visible: propId }`

## Idempotency
- Every build function must remove its own previous COMPONENT_SET + showcase FRAME first
- Also sweep loose orphan COMPONENTs by regex (cancelled builds leave them behind)

## Border colors (visual distinctness)
- Default = `border/strong` (visible neutral)
- Hover = `text/secondary` (clearly darker than Default)
- Don't use `border/default` for input rests — too light, looks unintentional
"}

# Figma Plugin Build Patterns (OM Design System)

## Text styles & fonts
- Always `await figma.loadFontAsync(style.fontName)` before `setTextStyleIdAsync()` AND before setting `.characters`
- NEVER set `text.fontName = {...}` AFTER `setTextStyleIdAsync()` — it breaks the typography link in the design panel
- If you want a specific weight, fix it on the text style itself (don't override on the node)

## Sizing & autolayout gotchas
- Set `layoutSizingHorizontal/Vertical` AFTER `appendChild()` — before that the parent context is unknown
- For frames that should keep a fixed counter-axis after children added, set `counterAxisSizingMode = 'FIXED'` then `layoutSizingVertical = 'FIXED'` and `.resize()` AT THE END (children may force a re-hug otherwise)

## Variable resolution (dynamic-page mode)
- Brand tokens (`brand/*`) live in `_Theme`, not `_Appearance`
- Use a fallback resolver: `anyBy = (n) => appBy(n) || thBy(n)`
- Always `await figma.variables.getLocalVariableCollectionsAsync()` (sync APIs disabled with `documentAccess: dynamic-page`)

## Component-as-instance pattern (atomic composition)
- For atoms reused inside larger components (Label inside TextField, Chip inside Dropdown):
  1. Build the atom first with its own component-set + state variants
  2. Lookup helper: `findXComponent(state)` → searches Atoms page for the COMPONENT_SET, returns the matching variant
  3. Throw `Run "Build X" first` if missing — clear error
  4. Instance via `comp.createInstance()`, name it, then `layoutSizingHorizontal = 'HUG'`
  5. Wire boolean props on the wrapper to toggle the entire instance via `componentPropertyReferences = { visible: propId }`

## Idempotency
- Every build function must remove its own previous COMPONENT_SET + showcase FRAME first
- Also sweep loose orphan COMPONENTs by regex (cancelled builds leave them behind)

## Border colors (visual distinctness)
- Default = `border/strong` (visible neutral)
- Hover = `text/secondary` (clearly darker than Default)
- Don't use `border/default` for input rests — too light, looks unintentional
PATTERNS_EOF

## Status (as of last session)

### ✅ Completed
- Phase 1.1: pages, collections, text styles
- Phase 2: 383 icons as components
- Phase 3 atoms:
  - Button (~440 variants)
  - Checkbox (108)

  - Avatar Single + Group (~75)

- Tooltip Heading variant (TOOLTIP_HEADINGS declared but rendering missing in `makeTooltipVariant`)
- Divider
- Spinner

### ⏳ Phase 4 — Molecules
Menu Item → Dropdown Menu → Select → Search Bar → Tabs → ...

### ⏳ Phase 5 — Motion / Interactions
Deferred to end of Phase 5.

## Locked design rules — Form atoms (May 6 2026)

- Sizes match Button: XS=24, Small=28, Medium=32, Default=36 (no Large)
- Padding: 8px all sizes
- Vertical gap (Label ↔ Field ↔ Helper): 2px
- Borders:
  - Default = `border/strong`
  - Hover = `text/secondary` (darker)
  - Active/Focus = `brand/primary` (no drop shadow)
- Helper text hidden by default
- Dropdown chips use neutral grey (`state/disabled-bg`), NOT brand colors
- Dropdown disabled state uses Disabled chip variant via `chipFor(size, state)`

## Form atom specs

```
FORM_SIZES = ['XS', 'Small', 'Medium', 'Default']
FORM_VGAP = 2
FORM_LABEL_STYLE = 'Label/Default'

FORM_SIZE_SPECS:
  XS:      { h:24, padX:8, icon:12, gap:6, radius:4 }
  Small:   { h:28, padX:8, icon:14, gap:6, radius:6 }
  Medium:  { h:32, padX:8, icon:14, gap:8, radius:6 }
  Default: { h:36, padX:8, icon:16, gap:8, radius:8 }

CHIP_SIZE_SPECS:
  Small:   { h:20, padX:6,  icon:12, text:'Body/Small',   radius:4 }
  Default: { h:28, padX:10, icon:14, text:'Body/Default', radius:6 }
```

## Critical fix notes

- Chip Default must use `comp.layoutSizingVertical='FIXED'; comp.resize(comp.width, spec.h)` AT END after appendChild calls (otherwise counter-axis hugs to text height = 20px regardless of Default's 28h)
- Dropdown field paddingTop/Bottom = 2 (was 6) so XS dropdown isn't crowded
"}

arguments.content:
# OM Design System — Build Plan

## Status (as of last session)

### ✅ Completed
- Phase 1.1: pages, collections, text styles
- Phase 2: 383 icons as components
- Phase 3 atoms:
  - Button (~440 variants)
  - Checkbox (108)
  - Radio (72)
  - Toggle (72)
  - Badge (~120)
  - Tooltip (32)
  - Avatar Single + Group (~75)
  - Label (2)
  - Chip (4)
  - TextField (42)
  - Textarea (14)
  - Dropdown (~150)

### ⏳ Pending atoms
- **Tag** (NEXT) — status colors: Neutral / Info / Success / Warning / Danger × 2 sizes × Solid / Outline / Subtle. Different from greyish Chip (which is ONLY for Multi-Select dropdowns).
- Tooltip Heading variant (TOOLTIP_HEADINGS declared but rendering missing in `makeTooltipVariant`)
- Divider
- Spinner

### ⏳ Phase 4 — Molecules
Menu Item → Dropdown Menu → Select → Search Bar → Tabs → ...

### ⏳ Phase 5 — Motion / Interactions
Deferred to end of Phase 5.

## Locked design rules — Form atoms (May 6 2026)

- Sizes match Button: XS=24, Small=28, Medium=32, Default=36 (no Large)
- Padding: 8px all sizes
- Vertical gap (Label ↔ Field ↔ Helper): 2px
- Borders:
  - Default = `border/strong`
  - Hover = `text/secondary` (darker)
  - Active/Focus = `brand/primary` (no drop shadow)
- Helper text hidden by default
- Dropdown chips use neutral grey (`state/disabled-bg`), NOT brand colors
- Dropdown disabled state uses Disabled chip variant via `chipFor(size, state)`

## Form atom specs

```
FORM_SIZES = ['XS', 'Small', 'Medium', 'Default']
FORM_VGAP = 2
FORM_LABEL_STYLE = 'Label/Default'

FORM_SIZE_SPECS:
  XS:      { h:24, padX:8, icon:12, gap:6, radius:4 }
  Small:   { h:28, padX:8, icon:14, gap:6, radius:6 }
  Medium:  { h:32, padX:8, icon:14, gap:8, radius:6 }
  Default: { h:36, padX:8, icon:16, gap:8, radius:8 }

CHIP_SIZE_SPECS:
  Small:   { h:20, padX:6,  icon:12, text:'Body/Small',   radius:4 }
  Default: { h:28, padX:10, icon:14, text:'Body/Default', radius:6 }
```

## Critical fix notes

- Chip Default must use `comp.layoutSizingVertical='FIXED'; comp.resize(comp.width, spec.h)` AT END after appendChild calls (otherwise counter-axis hugs to text height = 20px regardless of Default's 28h)
- Dropdown field paddingTop/Bottom = 2 (was 6) so XS dropdown isn't crowded
